name: OrKa Brain v2 Benchmark Dataset
description: Comprehensive benchmark evaluating Brain v2 skills + GraphScout routing vs stateless baseline. Five tracks: cross-domain transfer, anti-pattern avoidance, GraphScout routing, multi-skill composition, and longitudinal learning curves. 250 tasks x 2 conditions = 500+ execution runs.

A.name: Cross-Domain Skill Transfer
A.description: Learn procedural patterns in seed domains, then test transfer to 40 novel domains. Measures whether brain-recalled skills improve output quality in unseen contexts.

B.name: Anti-Pattern Avoidance
B.description: Seed 10 anti-patterns, then present 40 tasks that could trigger them. Measures whether the brain prevents known bad patterns.

C.name: GraphScout Brain-Assisted Routing
C.description: Seed path skills, then use GraphScout with brain_assisted=true for routing decisions. Measures routing quality and confidence with vs without brain path knowledge.

D.name: Multi-Skill Composition
D.description: Tasks requiring recall and composition of multiple skill types (recipe + anti-pattern + path). Measures combined skill application.

E.name: Longitudinal Learning Curve
E.description: 50 sequential software engineering tasks in the same domain. Measures whether brain condition improves over time via skill accumulation and feedback loops.

tasks.title: Learn: Extract-Transform-Load Pipeline
tasks.task: Design and implement an ETL pipeline that extracts data from multiple CSV sources, transforms it by cleaning nulls, normalizing date formats, and deduplicating records, then loads the results into a structured data warehouse schema.

steps.action: identify and catalog all data sources with schema inspection
extract raw data with error handling for malformed rows
apply type coercion and null-handling transformations
normalize date formats to ISO 8601 across all sources
deduplicate records using composite key matching
validate transformed data against target schema constraints
load validated records into warehouse with transaction safety

tasks.title: Learn: Scan-Classify-Remediate Vulnerability Flow
tasks.task: Scan an application for security vulnerabilities, classify each finding by CVSS severity and exploitability, then generate prioritized remediation plans with code-level fix suggestions.

steps.action: run static analysis scanners against the codebase
run dynamic analysis and penetration test probes
classify each finding by CVSS score and exploitability
prioritize findings combining severity and business impact
generate remediation plan with code-level fixes for each finding
validate remediation suggestions against security best practices

tasks.title: Learn: Research-Draft-Refine Content Creation
tasks.task: Research a topic thoroughly, draft long-form content with proper structure and evidence, then iteratively refine it based on readability metrics, SEO optimization, and audience engagement criteria.

steps.action: research topic gathering authoritative sources and key data points
create content outline with logical section progression
draft each section integrating evidence and examples
evaluate draft against readability and SEO metrics
refine weak sections while preserving content strengths
final review for consistency and audience alignment

tasks.title: Learn: Monitor-Diagnose-Recover Incident Response
tasks.task: Monitor system health metrics, diagnose the root cause of performance degradation by correlating logs across services, then execute a recovery plan with rollback safety.

steps.action: collect and aggregate health metrics from all monitored services
detect anomalies using baseline deviation analysis
correlate anomalies with log events across affected services
isolate root cause through dependency graph traversal
design recovery plan with rollback checkpoints
execute recovery with continuous health monitoring

tasks.title: Learn: Analyze-Generate-Validate Test Design
tasks.task: Analyze a software specification for testable requirements, generate comprehensive test cases with boundary conditions, then validate test coverage completeness and independence.

steps.action: parse specification extracting functional and non-functional requirements
identify boundary conditions and edge cases for each requirement
generate test cases covering normal, boundary, and error paths
assess coverage matrix against requirement traceability
validate test independence and eliminate redundant cases
prioritize test cases by risk and business impact

tasks.title: Learn: Scope-Estimate-Plan Project Planning
tasks.task: Define project scope from stakeholder requirements, estimate effort using historical data and complexity analysis, then create a realistic schedule with risk buffers and dependency chains.

steps.action: gather and categorize stakeholder requirements by priority
define scope boundaries with explicit in-scope and out-of-scope items
decompose scope into work breakdown structure tasks
estimate effort using complexity-weighted historical comparisons
identify task dependencies and critical path
build schedule with risk buffers on critical path items

tasks.title: Learn: Explore-Model-Evaluate ML Pipeline
tasks.task: Explore a dataset through statistical profiling and visualization, select and train appropriate models with hyperparameter tuning, then evaluate performance using cross-validation and interpretability analysis.

steps.action: profile dataset statistics: distributions, correlations, missing values
engineer features based on domain knowledge and statistical analysis
select candidate model architectures based on data characteristics
train models with hyperparameter optimization using cross-validation
evaluate model performance on held-out test set with multiple metrics
generate interpretability analysis: feature importance, SHAP values

tasks.title: Learn: Audit-Assess-Report Compliance Review
tasks.task: Audit organizational processes against regulatory requirements, assess compliance gaps with risk scoring, then produce a structured report with remediation timelines and evidence mapping.

steps.action: catalog applicable regulatory requirements and controls
audit current processes against each control requirement
identify compliance gaps and score risk exposure
design remediation actions for each gap with priority ranking
map evidence to controls for audit trail completeness
produce structured compliance report with executive summary

tasks.title: Learn: Discover-Prototype-Test User-Centered Design
tasks.task: Discover user needs through research synthesis, prototype interface solutions matching those needs, then test prototypes with usability heuristics and predict user behavior patterns.

steps.action: synthesize user research data into personas and journey maps
identify key pain points and opportunity areas from research
generate design concepts addressing identified needs
prototype highest-potential concepts with interaction flows
evaluate prototypes against Nielsen usability heuristics
predict user behavior patterns and iterate on weak areas

tasks.title: Learn: Gather-Structure-Polish Documentation
tasks.task: Gather technical information from code and interviews, structure it into a logical documentation hierarchy, then polish with examples, cross-references, and consistency checks.

steps.action: extract technical details from codebase and API signatures
interview domain experts for context and use cases
organize information into a logical hierarchy with clear sections
write each section with code examples and diagrams
add cross-references and navigation aids between sections
review for consistency, accuracy, and completeness

tasks.title: Transfer: Provenance Attribution of Looted Antiquities
tasks.task: Ingest spectroscopic mineral-composition readings from 23 confiscated pottery shards across 4 forensic labs (each lab uses a different instrument calibration and reporting schema), reconcile overlapping wavelength ranges, normalize measurement units from absorbance to reflectance percentages, deduplicate shards that were sampled twice under different accession numbers, and load the harmonized dataset into a provenance-attribution GIS layer for court exhibit preparation. Two of the labs deliver results as nested XML with inline calibration metadata, one delivers fixed-width FORTRAN output, and one delivers handwritten PDF scans that have been OCR'd with ~8% character error rate.

tasks.title: Transfer: Livestock Disease Surveillance Consolidation
tasks.task: Extract herd-level disease test results from 14 regional veterinary laboratories that each use a different LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) with varying field definitions for 'positive', 'suspect', and 'negative'. Transform results by reconciling species-breed taxonomies against the FAO DAD-IS nomenclature, converting geo-coordinates from 6 different CRS projections to WGS-84, flagging incongruent test-retest pairs where the same animal was tested at two labs within 30 days with conflicting outcomes, and load the unified surveillance dataset into an OIE-compatible WAHIS reporting schema while preserving chain-of-custody metadata for each sample.

tasks.title: Transfer: Hull & Machinery Underwriting Risk Scan
tasks.task: Scan a commercial shipping fleet's maintenance dossiers (a mix of Classification Society survey PDFs, Port State Control deficiency databases, ISM Code audit reports, and AIS tracking anomaly logs) for latent structural and operational risks. Classify each finding using the IACS Common Structural Rules severity taxonomy cross-referenced with P&I Club loss-history actuarial bands, factoring in vessel age, flag-state detention rates, and crew competency certificate expiry proximity. Generate a prioritized risk mitigation schedule that sequences dry-dock interventions, crew training mandates, and voyage-specific trading restrictions, with cost-benefit analysis per intervention.

tasks.title: Transfer: Digital Preservation Vulnerability Assessment
tasks.task: Scan a national archive's 2.3PB digital preservation repository for format obsolescence risks, bit-rot indicators, and metadata schema drift across 40 years of heterogeneous ingest. Classify each at-risk object by the NDSA Levels of Digital Preservation maturity framework, severity-weighted by cultural significance (UNESCO Memory of the World criteria) and legal deposit obligations. Recommend format migration paths with fidelity-loss estimates, prioritized by a composite score combining irreplaceability, access frequency, and format community support trajectory. Two collection series use proprietary 1990s-era formats where the vendor no longer exists.

tasks.title: Transfer: Ecclesiastical Chant Restoration Brief
tasks.task: Research the historically-informed performance practice for a fragmentary 11th-century Aquitanian neume manuscript (3 of 7 folios survive) by cross-referencing Solesmes transcriptions, the Graduale Triplex, and recent palaeographic studies disputing the rhythmic interpretation. Draft a critical performing edition with apparatus noting variant readings, editorial emendations (using square brackets / italics conventions), and a 2000-word preface situating the repertoire within the liturgical calendar of the Cluniac reform. Refine based on peer review by a musicologist specializing in modal theory who objects to your transcription of a quilisma passage on folio 3r.

tasks.title: Transfer: Orbital Debris Liability Framework
tasks.task: Research the intersection of the 1972 Liability Convention, ITU Radio Regulations, and emerging national space legislation (US ORBITS Act draft, Luxembourg Space Resources Law, UAE Space Agency Decree) as they apply to a proposed active debris removal mission targeting 5 defunct satellites owned by 3 different nations. Draft a legal framework document addressing (a) fault vs absolute liability allocation for secondary debris creation during capture, (b) intellectual property concerns if proprietary satellite components are retrieved, and (c) insurance structuring for a mission with no actuarial precedent. Refine after the client's risk committee challenges your treatment of the Cosmos 954 precedent and requests alternative liability cap structures.

tasks.title: Transfer: Recirculating Aquaculture System Crisis Response
tasks.task: Monitor dissolved oxygen, ammonia/nitrite, pH, and temperature sensor arrays across 48 tanks in a land-based Atlantic salmon RAS facility. Diagnose the root cause of a sudden 40% mortality spike in cohort tanks 12-18 by correlating biofilter nitrification efficiency logs, ozone dosing records, feed conversion ratio trends, and a water chemistry anomaly that only appeared in the post-UV treatment stage. The facility's SCADA system logs are in 15-minute intervals but the mortality event unfolded over ~90 minutes between scheduled readings. Execute a recovery plan involving emergency water exchanges, biofilter bypass, and staged re-introduction of surviving stock to clean tanks, with continuous pathogen sampling to rule out VHS/IHN viral co-factors.

tasks.title: Transfer: Synchrotron Beamline Quench Recovery
tasks.task: Monitor beam current, magnet temperatures, vacuum pressures, and cryogenic helium levels across 240 superconducting dipole magnets in a synchrotron storage ring. Diagnose a cascading quench event that propagated through 8 consecutive arc cells by correlating quench protection system firing sequences (sub-millisecond timestamps), cryogenic valve response logs, beam loss monitor spike patterns, and the preceding 72 hours of slowly drifting orbit corrector currents that suggest a pre-existing alignment issue. Execute magnet training recovery: staged re-cooling, incremental current ramp with quench margin testing, beam threading at reduced energy, and a root-cause report distinguishing between a training quench, a conductor defect, and a cryogenic system instability.

tasks.title: Transfer: ETCS Level 2 Signalling Test Campaign
tasks.task: Analyze the ERTMS/ETCS Level 2 System Requirements Specification (Subset-026) for a 140km high-speed rail corridor carrying both domestic EMUs and cross-border locomotive-hauled services. Generate test cases covering: movement authority shortening during emergency braking, RBC-RBC handover at national border with differing track-side engineering rules, degraded mode fallback from Level 2 to Level 1 with infill loops, simultaneous axle counter reset during track section occupancy, and national value discrepancies in brake deceleration curves between the two countries' infrastructure managers. Validate test completeness against the ERA reference test specification and ensure traceability to each SRS requirement clause.

tasks.title: Transfer: EUV Lithography Process Window Qualification
tasks.task: Analyze the process specification for a 3nm GAA-FET gate patterning step using EUV lithography (13.5nm wavelength, 0.33 NA). Generate qualification test cases covering: critical dimension uniformity across the full reticle field (33x26mm) with ≤0.5nm 3σ tolerance, stochastic defect sensitivity at sub-30 photon/nm² dose for line-edge roughness, mask 3D effects from absorber shadowing at 6° chief ray angle, focus-dose matrix (Bossung plot) coverage including forbidden pitch interactions, and overlay registration to the prior metal layer across wafer bow and thermal chuck signature variations. Validate that the test suite covers all process failure modes catalogued in the fab's historical yield-loss Pareto and that no test case assumes availability of offline metrology tools during high-volume manufacturing.

tasks.title: Transfer: Post-Earthquake Emergency Shelter Program
tasks.task: Define scope for a 90-day emergency shelter program serving 45,000 displaced persons across 12 sites following a 7.2 magnitude earthquake in a landlocked mountainous country. Estimate effort for shelter kit procurement (considering that the two nearest regional stockpiles are depleted and sea freight takes 6 weeks), rubble clearance with explosive ordnance contamination assessment, site preparation on unstable terrain, and winterization given that nighttime temperatures will drop below -5°C within 45 days. Create a schedule with inter-site dependency chains (some sites accessible only after road clearance at upstream sites), customs clearance uncertainties, and workforce availability buffers accounting for 20% of local labor force being displaced themselves. The schedule must satisfy Sphere Standards minimum shelter area (3.5m² per person) verification gates.

tasks.title: Transfer: Floating Offshore Wind Farm Installation Campaign
tasks.task: Define scope for the installation of 80 semi-submersible floating wind turbines (15MW each) in a deepwater site 60km offshore with a challenging metocean window (wave height >2.5m excludes heavy-lift vessel operations 55% of the year). Estimate mobilization durations for the specialized fleet (semi-submersible heavy transport vessel, anchor handling tug supply vessels, cable-lay vessel with 5-month lead time), factoring in port assembly quayside constraints where only 3 floaters can be simultaneously assembled. Plan the 30-month installation timeline with weather window stochastic modeling, inter-turbine dynamic cable pull-in sequence dependencies, anchor pre-lay campaigns that must avoid herring spawning closures, and grid connection milestones that trigger penalty clauses if missed by more than 60 days.

tasks.title: Transfer: Ice Core Proxy Calibration Pipeline
tasks.task: Explore a multi-proxy paleoclimate dataset combining δ¹⁸O isotope ratios, dust particle concentrations, methane mixing ratios, and annual layer thickness measurements from a 3,200m Antarctic ice core spanning 800,000 years. Profile the dataset for: measurement gaps where brittle-zone ice crumbled during extraction (depth 600-900m), systematic calibration offsets between three labs that measured adjacent core sections, and age-model uncertainties compounding at depth where annual layers are compressed below instrument resolution. Train a Bayesian age-depth model integrating volcanic tie-points from the GICC05 chronology with Monte Carlo sampling of stratigraphic uncertainty, then evaluate model skill using leave-one-out cross-validation against independently-dated tephra layers, reporting posterior credible intervals rather than point estimates.

tasks.title: Transfer: Bridge Fatigue Life Prognosis
tasks.task: Explore 15 years of continuous strain gauge, accelerometer, and temperature sensor data from 128 measurement points on a steel box-girder highway bridge carrying 40,000 vehicles/day. Profile sensor degradation patterns (drift, dropout, aliasing from loose connections), traffic load cycle distributions using rainflow counting, and thermal strain isolation from traffic-induced mechanical strain. Train a fatigue damage accumulation model (Miner's rule with S-N curve Bayesian updating) that accounts for weld detail category uncertainty and corrosion progression, incorporating climate-change-adjusted thermal cycling projections. Evaluate remaining fatigue life predictions against recent inspection findings of two newly-discovered 15mm weld toe cracks at diaphragm connections, computing probability-of-failure curves for 10/25/50-year horizons.

tasks.title: Transfer: EASA Part-145 Maintenance Organization Audit
tasks.task: Audit a Part-145 aircraft maintenance organization against EASA Regulation (EU) 2021/1963 and the associated AMC/GM material, covering 12 workshops across 3 countries operating under a single approval. Assess compliance gaps in: human factors training program currency (noting that 15% of certifying staff have expired CRM modules), tool calibration traceability across workshops that share pooled equipment, non-conformance management where 40 open findings span 3 competent authority inspections, and EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) maintenance data integrity where a recent software update introduced rendering errors in AMM diagrams. Produce a corrective action plan satisfying EASA standardization inspection expectations, with evidence mapping showing which objective evidence package closes each finding, and a risk-prioritized timeline that front-loads anything affecting continued airworthiness.

tasks.title: Transfer: Nuclear Facility Decommissioning Regulatory Assessment
tasks.task: Audit a gas-cooled reactor decommissioning project against IAEA Safety Standards (GSR Part 6), national nuclear regulatory requirements, and OSPAR Convention obligations for marine discharge. Assess compliance gaps in: radioactive waste characterization where 30% of the graphite moderator core has activation levels exceeding the facility's interim storage WAC (Waste Acceptance Criteria), decommissioning worker dose budgets that are tracking 15% above ALARA optimization targets due to unexpected hot-spots behind biological shielding, stakeholder engagement deficiencies where the local community liaison committee has formally objected to the Environmental Impact Assessment timeline, and the site end-state definition where 'green field' vs 'brown field' criteria remain unresolved between the licensee and regulator. Produce a regulatory submission report with remediation timelines aligned to the national radioactive waste management program's GDF availability schedule.

tasks.title: Transfer: AAC Device Interaction Model for Non-Verbal Users
tasks.task: Discover the interaction needs of 5 non-verbal user personas with different motor capabilities (eye-gaze only, single-switch scanning, head-tracking with tremor, partner-assisted scanning, and direct touch with limited fine motor) for an Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) device. Prototype a unified interaction framework that supports all 5 input modalities simultaneously with graceful degradation when combined with environmental interference (IR lighting washing out eye-gaze cameras, electromagnetic interference from hospital equipment disrupting Bluetooth switches). Test each prototype against RESNA AT outcome measurement frameworks and predict vocabulary selection speed (words/minute) and user fatigue onset timing for each persona under simulated conversation scenarios requiring both pre-stored phrases and novel word construction.

tasks.title: Transfer: Emergency Alert System User Interface
tasks.task: Discover the information needs of 4 distinct population segments during a compound disaster (simultaneous earthquake + chemical plant explosion): elderly residents with low digital literacy, parents of school-age children needing evacuation routing, first responders needing real-time hazard perimeter updates, and hearing-impaired individuals who cannot receive audio alerts. Prototype a multi-modal alert interface that delivers differentiated, actionable information through push notifications, television override, outdoor siren-plus-LED networks, and a web dashboard — all within 90 seconds of alert issuance. Test using FEMA IPAWS integration standards, Nielsen heuristics adapted for panic-state cognitive load, and predict behavioral compliance rates for each segment using Protection Motivation Theory frameworks.

tasks.title: Transfer: Endangered Language Documentation Toolkit
tasks.task: Gather linguistic field recordings, interlinear glossed texts, and paradigm elicitation data from a Papuan language with no prior written documentation and fewer than 200 speakers, sourced from 6 fieldworkers using 4 different transcription conventions (IPA narrow, IPA broad, practical orthography, ad-hoc romanization). Structure the materials into an ELAR/PARADISEC-compatible archive with FLEx-interoperable lexical database, grammatical sketch organized by typological feature (morphophonology, argument structure, clause combining, and information structure), and curated text collection with time-aligned audio. Polish with cross-references between lexical entries and text attestations, consistency validation of morpheme segmentation across transcribers, and a metadata layer capturing speaker consent status, recording conditions, and dialectal variation notes.

tasks.title: Transfer: Natural History Collection Digitization Manual
tasks.task: Gather specimen handling protocols from 8 curatorial departments (entomology, mammalogy, herpetology, paleobotany, mineralogy, invertebrate zoology, ichthyology, ornithology) each with different preservation methods (pinned, fluid-preserved, freeze-dried, fossil matrix, thin-section, skeletal, study skin), imaging requirements (macro stacking, UV fluorescence, CT scanning, RTI), and metadata standards (Darwin Core, ABCD Schema, custom legacy databases). Structure into a unified digitization operations manual with department-specific workflows, a decision tree for imaging method selection based on specimen type/condition, equipment calibration checklists, and data quality validation rules. Polish with cross-references between specimen types that require multiple imaging passes, terminology glossary reconciling departmental naming conflicts, and quality assurance protocols for the 3 outsourced digitization vendors who will use this manual.

tasks.title: Transfer: Subsea Production System Telemetry Integration
tasks.task: Extract condition-monitoring telemetry from 6 subsea Christmas tree control modules (each vendor uses a proprietary Modbus register map with different byte-ordering conventions), 4 pipeline SCADA historians with overlapping but inconsistent tag naming, 2 ROV inspection video analytics systems producing structured anomaly JSON at different confidence thresholds, and a marine growth monitoring sonar system delivering bathymetric point clouds in vendor-specific binary format. Transform by reconciling equipment tag IDs against the facility's ISO 14224 equipment hierarchy, converting all timestamps to UTC (sources span 3 time zones and one system uses GPS week numbers), normalizing pressure readings to a common reference datum, and flagging readings where sensor drift exceeds API 17F calibration thresholds. Load into an asset integrity management platform that requires a specific WITSML-like schema.

tasks.title: Transfer: Old Master Painting Forensic Analysis
tasks.task: Scan a disputed Old Master painting using a battery of non-invasive and micro-invasive techniques (X-ray fluorescence mapping, infrared reflectography, dendrochronology of the panel, cross-section polarized light microscopy, and Raman spectroscopy of binding media). Classify each finding against the known material palette of the attributed artist's authenticated works, severity-weighted by a 'anachronism score' that flags materials not commercially available before the purported date (e.g., zinc white before 1780, synthetic ultramarine before 1828). Some findings are ambiguous: the ground layer composition matches both the attributed artist and their known copyist from 50 years later. Generate an authentication report with confidence intervals, recommending additional tests (14C dating of binding media, lead isotope analysis) where current evidence is inconclusive, structured to withstand cross-examination in an art fraud proceeding.

tasks.title: Transfer: Pharmaceutical Patent Landscape Analysis
tasks.task: Research the global patent landscape for a novel bispecific antibody targeting PD-L1/TIGIT in solid tumors, covering USPTO, EPO, JPO, CNIPA, and KIPO databases. Prior art includes 2,300+ relevant patent families with complex claim dependency chains, continuation-in-part filings, and 14 interference/opposition proceedings across jurisdictions. Draft a freedom-to-operate opinion addressing (a) whether the client's specific CDR sequences fall within pending Markush claim boundaries, (b) a Supplementary Protection Certificate extension that shifts expiry dates in 6 EU member states, and (c) a compulsory licensing risk under TRIPS Article 31 in 3 target markets. Refine after in-house counsel identifies that one key blocking patent has an overlooked terminal disclaimer that changes the enforceability analysis, and the R&D team reveals a manufacturing process change that may trigger a new prior art reference.

tasks.title: Transfer: Radio Telescope Array Correlator Failure Recovery
tasks.task: Monitor 64 antenna stations' phase stability, system temperatures, RFI (radio frequency interference) flagging rates, and correlator node health across a synthesis imaging array during a 12-hour VLBI observation session. Diagnose the cause of sudden coherence loss on baselines involving antennas 23-31 by correlating correlator GPU node memory error logs, hydrogen maser frequency standard drift measurements, fiber optic signal-to-noise degradation patterns, and weather station wind speed data (antenna pointing accuracy degrades above 12 m/s). The coherence loss pattern is asymmetric — only East-West baselines are affected, suggesting either a clock offset or an ionospheric phase screen. Execute recovery: re-derive delay models from a calibrator source observation, implement fringe-fitting corrections to salvage the partially corrupted data, switch to backup correlator nodes, and adjust the remaining schedule to re-observe the most severely affected target fields during the next available LST window.

tasks.title: Transfer: AV Perception Stack Edge Case Test Suite
tasks.task: Analyze the perception stack specification for an L4 autonomous vehicle operating in a mixed urban/highway ODD (Operational Design Domain), covering camera, LiDAR, radar, and ultrasonic sensor fusion with a 67-class object taxonomy. Generate test cases covering: simultaneous occlusion of a cyclist by a bus while the LiDAR returns are split by a chain-link fence (partial detection ambiguity), radar ghost targets from multi-path reflections off highway sound barriers during rain, camera autoexposure hunting when transitioning from a tunnel to direct sunlight while a pedestrian in dark clothing is in the crosswalk, and IMU drift accumulation during GPS-denied traversal of a 2km urban canyon. Validate test coverage against ISO 21448 (SOTIF) known and unknown unsafe scenarios matrix, ASAM OpenSCENARIO conformance, and ensure no test case assumes sensor performance beyond the manufacturer's published specifications for the installed hardware revision.

tasks.title: Transfer: Multi-Period Archaeological Excavation Schedule
tasks.task: Define scope for a 6-month rescue excavation of a multi-period site (Neolithic, Roman, Medieval) ahead of a highway construction deadline that is legally immovable. Estimate excavation rates accounting for: a waterlogged Roman harbor deposit requiring on-site conservation facilities and specialist environmental sampling, an unexpectedly dense Medieval cemetery (estimated 400+ inhumations, each requiring individual osteological recording and potential DNA sampling per government repatriation protocols), and the discovery of a probable Neolithic henge ditch that extends beyond the planning boundary requiring emergency supplementary consent from the local authority. Build the excavation schedule with weather-dependent soil condition constraints (clay context excavation halts when ground is frozen or waterlogged), specialist subcontractor availability windows (the only available osteoarchaeologist can work weeks 8-14 only), artifact processing bottleneck buffers, and regulatory milestones (post-excavation assessment must be submitted within 12 months per planning condition).

tasks.title: Transfer: Eruption Forecasting Ensemble Model
tasks.task: Explore a multi-parameter volcano monitoring dataset combining seismicity catalogs (VT and LP event counts, RSAM amplitudes), ground deformation (InSAR, tiltmeter, continuous GPS), SO₂ and CO₂ flux measurements (DOAS traverses and MultiGAS), thermal infrared anomaly maps, and historical eruption records spanning 300 years with variable data quality. Profile the dataset for: seismometer saturation periods during swarm activity, GPS antenna offsets from lahar damage to monuments, gas measurement gaps during adverse wind conditions, and historical records that conflate eruption parameters from eyewitness accounts of unknown reliability. Train an ensemble forecasting model combining a Bayesian Event Tree (BET_EF) with machine-learning precursory pattern recognition, then evaluate forecast skill using retrospective hindcasting against the 4 known eruptions in the historical record, reporting probability gain over a simple base-rate model and calculating Brier skill scores per forecast window (24h, 7d, 30d).

tasks.title: Transfer: International Fisheries Compliance Audit (RFMO)
tasks.task: Audit a coastal nation's fisheries management program against the Port State Measures Agreement (PSMA), FAO Code of Conduct for Responsible Fisheries, and the regional RFMO conservation and management measures for 3 straddling fish stocks. Assess compliance gaps in: VMS (Vessel Monitoring System) coverage where 12% of the industrial fleet operates in a satellite shadow zone near the continental shelf edge, observer program coverage that falls below the RFMO-mandated 20% for longline vessels due to observer safety concerns on certain flag-of-convenience vessels, transhipment monitoring where 8 at-sea transhipment events lacked complete product verification, and IUU (Illegal Unreported Unregulated) vessel list cross-checking procedures that have a 72-hour processing lag against the RFMO's real-time vessel registry updates. Produce an audit report structured for the FAO IPOA-IUU framework, mapping each gap to a corrective action with implementation timeline and capacity-building assistance requirements the nation may request from the RFMO cooperative fund.

tasks.title: Transfer: Variable-Rate Irrigation Control Interface
tasks.task: Discover the decision-support needs of 3 user personas (farm manager overseeing 2000 hectares across 8 pivots, agronomist consulting for 15 farms remotely, and the pivot equipment operator in the field with limited connectivity) for a variable-rate irrigation system that must synthesize soil moisture sensor arrays, NDVI satellite imagery (5-day revisit), weather forecast APIs, and real-time flow meter data. Prototype a multi-modal interface: a desktop planning view for pre-season prescription map creation, a mobile field view with offline capability showing prescription overlays on GPS-located pivot position, and an automated alert system for sensor exceedance events. Test against ISO 11783 (ISOBUS) display ergonomics standards, predict water savings efficiency compared to uniform-rate irrigation using the site's 3-year historical soil moisture variability, and evaluate cognitive switching costs when the agronomist must rapidly context-switch between 4 farms during a heat event.

tasks.title: Transfer: Incunabula Cataloging Standard Operating Procedures
tasks.task: Gather cataloging protocols from 5 rare book libraries (each using different combinations of DCRM(B), ISBD(A), ISTC identifiers, and local shelf-mark systems), expert knowledge from 3 bibliographers with conflicting views on fingerprint formula methodology, provenance chain documentation standards from the Consortium of European Research Libraries, and condition assessment guidelines from the Institute of Conservation that use a different severity scale than the library's existing condition grades. Structure into a unified SOP with decision trees for: distinguishing issue/state/variant at the copy level, recording binding evidence chronologically when a volume has been rebound multiple times, and handling cataloging conflicts where ISTC and GW assign different editions to copies with identical collational formulae. Polish with cross-references between physical bibliography terminology and RDA/MARC field mappings, worked examples photographed from the collection, and a concordance table resolving the 4 different condition grading scales into a single normalized schema.

tasks.title: Transfer: Multi-Element Assay Database Harmonization
tasks.task: Extract geochemical assay results from 11 drilling campaigns spanning 30 years across a polymetallic deposit (Cu-Au-Mo-Ag), where each campaign used different analytical methods (fire assay, 4-acid digest ICP-MS, aqua regia ICP-OES, pXRF field screening) with different detection limits, digestion completeness for refractory minerals, and quality control protocols (some campaigns lack any blank/standard/duplicate data). Transform by harmonizing detection limits using regression-based left-censored data imputation, applying interlaboratory bias corrections derived from paired umpire re-assay programs, converting legacy imperial-unit collar surveys to metric with datum transformation from NAD27 to NAD83, and flagging samples where silver assays from the aqua regia digest likely under-report due to incomplete dissolution of argentojarosite. Load into a JORC/NI 43-101 compliant resource estimation database that requires classification of each sample by analytical reliability tier.

tasks.title: Transfer: Historic Textile Collection Risk Survey
tasks.task: Scan a museum textile collection of 4,500 objects (tapestries, garments, ecclesiastical vestments, ethnographic textiles) for deterioration risks using a combination of visual assessment with raking light, fiber identification by PLM, pH indicator strips on accessible areas, and micro-fading testing on representative dye classes. Classify each risk by a composite score using the CCI Framework for Preserving Heritage Collections (10 agents of deterioration), cross-referenced with the object's exhibition/loan demand forecast and insurance valuation. Generate a prioritized conservation treatment and storage improvement plan that accounts for: the conservation lab's 18-month treatment backlog, 6 objects with active insect infestation requiring immediate isolation, 12 light-sensitive objects currently displayed above recommended lux levels in a gallery that cannot be modified for 2 years due to a donor naming agreement, and a budget that can fund treatment of only 300 objects in the next fiscal year.

tasks.title: Transfer: Constitutional Commerce Clause Brief
tasks.task: Research the dormant Commerce Clause jurisprudence as it applies to a state law imposing differential tax treatment on out-of-state data center operators versus in-state operators (a novel extension of tax discrimination doctrine to cloud computing infrastructure). Prior precedent is sparse — only 3 circuit courts have addressed analogous digital economy Commerce Clause questions, with conflicting holdings. Draft an appellate brief arguing for strict scrutiny under the Pike balancing test, incorporating (a) empirical economic analysis of the tax's discriminatory burden, (b) a Compact Clause counter-argument the opposing side has previewed, and (c) dormant Commerce Clause originalist scholarship that your co-counsel (a constitutional law professor) insists must be addressed even though you believe it weakens the argument. Refine after the court issues a mid-briefing order requesting supplemental argument on whether the Internet Tax Freedom Act preempts the state law entirely — a theory neither party had briefed.

tasks.title: Transfer: Spacecraft Attitude Control Anomaly Resolution
tasks.task: Monitor a GEO communications satellite's attitude determination and control subsystem (star trackers, gyroscopes, sun sensors, reaction wheels, and Hall-effect thrusters) during a scheduled station-keeping maneuver. Diagnose why the spacecraft entered safe-hold mode 47 seconds into the maneuver by correlating: reaction wheel speed telemetry showing unexpected torque spikes, a star tracker exclusion zone event triggered by sun intrusion on the secondary tracker, gyroscope bias drift rates that diverged from the onboard calibration model by 0.003°/hr (within spec but at the 3σ boundary), and a 23-second telemetry gap from the ground station due to a transmitter waveguide fault. Two of the four reaction wheels have been operating in a degraded momentum management mode since a previous anomaly 8 months ago. Execute recovery: ground-commanded attitude re-acquisition using sun-sensor-only coarse pointing, gyroscope bias recalibration, reaction wheel momentum desaturation via thruster firing, and return to nominal attitude control mode with updated onboard autonomy parameters.

tasks.title: Transfer: Theranostic Radiopharmaceutical QA Test Protocol
tasks.task: Analyze the regulatory specifications for a novel Lutetium-177 PSMA theranostic radiopharmaceutical manufacturing process (GMP Annex 1 clean room requirements, Ph.Eur. monograph 2798 for Lu-177 chloride, FDA 21 CFR 212 for PET/therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals) where the 6.7-day half-life creates a hard constraint that all QC testing must complete within 4 hours of batch release. Generate test cases covering: radionuclidic purity assessment where the 5.5% Lu-177m metastable isomer content must be distinguished from Lu-177 ground state by gamma spectrometry with detector dead-time correction, radiochemical purity by HPLC where the acceptance criterion is ≥95% but the reference standard is unavailable (requiring system suitability workaround), sterility testing using a parametric release approach because the 14-day USP <71> test would expire the product, and endotoxin testing at the clinical dose volume where the maximum valid dilution calculation creates a mathematical conflict with the product's specific activity. Validate that no test case violates ALARA radiation protection principles for QC laboratory personnel and that the test sequence respects the physical decay clock.

tasks.title: Transfer: Major Film Archive Restoration Program Plan
tasks.task: Define scope for the digital restoration of 200 nitrate-era films (1910-1951) from a national film archive's vault, where 30% have active vinegar syndrome (acetic acid off-gassing causing autocatalytic base degradation), 15% exist only as duplication negatives with generation-loss artifacts, and 12 titles have no surviving original negative and must be reconstructed from multiple incomplete prints held by 4 different archives across 3 countries. Estimate effort for each restoration tier (preservation scan only, basic digital restoration, full 4K restoration with manual frame-by-frame repair), accounting for the variable scanning speed (acetate base vs nitrate base handling protocols), the specialized color grading expertise required for 2-strip Technicolor processes, and the intellectual property clearance process for 40 orphan works with uncertain rights holders. Build the schedule with film vault cold-storage cycling constraints (only 20 cans/day can be acclimated from 5°C vault to scanning suite temperature), scanner maintenance windows, and a grant funding disbursement schedule with milestone deliverables every 6 months.

tasks.title: Transfer: Induced Seismicity Traffic Light Model
tasks.task: Explore a dataset combining: microseismic monitoring catalogs from a dense surface array and downhole geophones near a wastewater disposal well (50,000+ events over 3 years with magnitude completeness varying from ML -1.0 to ML 0.5 depending on network configuration changes), injection rate/pressure/volume time series with 17 reporting gaps where the operator switched SCADA systems, regional stress-field orientation estimates from borehole breakout and focal mechanism inversions that disagree by 25°, and pore pressure diffusion modeling outputs calibrated to formation permeability values that span 2 orders of magnitude. Train a traffic-light protocol model that predicts the probability of an induced event exceeding ML 3.0 based on injection operational parameters, accounting for the non-stationary b-value evolution and Coulomb stress transfer from prior events. Evaluate model discrimination power using receiver operating characteristic curves against historical traffic-light threshold exceedances, and assess whether the model would have provided actionable warning before the 3 largest events in the catalog.

tasks.title: Transfer: WTO Dispute Settlement Compliance Audit
tasks.task: Audit a WTO member state's implementation of a Dispute Settlement Body ruling requiring modification of agricultural subsidies under the Agreement on Agriculture Annex 2 ('Green Box') criteria. Assess compliance gaps in: reclassification of 8 subsidy programs where the member claims they now meet Green Box Paragraph 2(b) decoupled income support criteria but 3 of the programs retain implicit production-linkage through eligibility conditions, scheduled tariff reductions that are technically compliant with bound rates but undercut by a new 'special safeguard' invocation that the complaining party argues is pretextual, cross-subsidization through state-owned enterprise procurement at above-market prices (which the member argues falls outside WTO agricultural subsidy disciplines), and an SPS measure on a key agricultural import that the ruling panel found to lack sufficient scientific risk assessment under Article 5.1. Produce an assessment report suitable for a compliance panel review, with legal citations to panel/AB reports, suggested Article 21.5 implementation options ranked by litigation risk, and a timeline for achieving full compliance before the reasonable period of time expires in 14 months.

tasks.title: Transfer: Underground Mine Ventilation Control Interface
tasks.task: Discover the operational needs of 3 user personas (mine ventilation engineer in the surface control room, underground shift supervisor carrying a ruggedized tablet, and the emergency response coordinator who may be off-site) for a ventilation-on-demand system in a 7-level underground mine with 45 independently-controlled fans, 30 regulators, and 120 environmental monitoring stations measuring airflow, methane, DPM, temperature, and humidity. Prototype a unified control interface that supports: predictive ventilation scheduling based on blasting cycles and truck dispatch routes, emergency ventilation reversal procedures that must display propagation timing (how long until fresh air reaches each refuge station), and energy optimization mode that reduces fan power during non-production shifts while maintaining minimum regulatory airflows. Test against IEC 62682 alarm management standards (to prevent alarm flooding during fan trip events), predict energy savings against the current fixed-speed fan operation baseline, and evaluate the interface's usability under simulated emergency conditions where smoke obscures one of the three display modalities.

tasks.title: Transfer: Forensic Accounting Investigation Documentation Standard
tasks.task: Gather evidence collection and documentation protocols from 4 different forensic accounting engagements (SEC enforcement referral, FCPA internal investigation, bankruptcy trustee avoidance action, and insurance fraud claim), where each followed different legal privilege frameworks (attorney-client, work product, joint defense agreement) that constrain how work papers can be organized and cross-referenced. Collect methodology standards from the ACFE Fraud Examiners Manual, AICPA forensic accounting guidelines, and the IBA Rules on the Taking of Evidence, noting conflicting guidance on statistical sampling adequacy thresholds. Structure into a reusable forensic investigation documentation SOP with: evidence chain-of-custody tracking that satisfies both civil litigation discovery obligations and potential criminal referral evidentiary standards, work paper organization that maintains privilege boundaries while allowing authorized parties to trace findings to source documents, and a tiered documentation protocol where the depth of analysis varies by materiality threshold (the US engagement uses $50k, the UK engagement uses £25k). Polish with cross-references between source document exhibits and finding summaries, a terminology concordance (US GAAP vs IFRS vs local statutory reporting terminology), and quality control reviewer checklists calibrated to the engagement's specific legal standard of proof.

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Premature Optimization

anti_pattern.what_failed: Optimizing code before profiling showed a bottleneck
anti_pattern.why: Wasted 3 days rewriting a function that accounted for 0.1% of execution time, introducing bugs in the process

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Ignoring Error Handling

anti_pattern.what_failed: Catching all exceptions with a bare except clause and logging nothing
anti_pattern.why: Silent failures masked a data corruption issue for 2 weeks before a customer reported invalid reports

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Monolithic Deployment

anti_pattern.what_failed: Deploying the entire application as a single artifact when only one module changed
anti_pattern.why: Full deployment took 45 minutes and required full regression testing, turning a 5-line bugfix into a 4-hour release cycle

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Hardcoded Configuration

anti_pattern.what_failed: Embedding database connection strings and API keys directly in source code
anti_pattern.why: Credentials leaked to GitHub, required emergency rotation of all production secrets and a security incident report

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: No Integration Tests

anti_pattern.what_failed: Relying solely on unit tests with mocked dependencies, never testing actual service interactions
anti_pattern.why: A serialization format change in an upstream service went undetected for a sprint, causing a production data loss incident

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Over-Engineering Abstractions

anti_pattern.what_failed: Creating a 5-layer abstraction hierarchy for a feature with exactly one implementation
anti_pattern.why: Added 800 lines of boilerplate, tripled onboarding time for new developers, and all predicted extension points were never used

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Unbounded Batch Processing

anti_pattern.what_failed: Processing an entire queue of messages in a single transaction without batch size limits
anti_pattern.why: Memory exhaustion crashed the worker when queue depth spiked to 500K messages during a marketing campaign

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Skipping Code Review for Hotfixes

anti_pattern.what_failed: Pushing a production hotfix directly to main without code review
anti_pattern.why: The fix introduced a SQL injection vulnerability that was exploited within 48 hours

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: N+1 Query Pattern

anti_pattern.what_failed: Loading child records one-by-one inside a loop instead of using batch/join queries
anti_pattern.why: API response time degraded from 200ms to 12 seconds as the dataset grew from 100 to 10K records

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Feature Flag Debt

anti_pattern.what_failed: Accumulating 200+ feature flags without a cleanup process or expiry dates
anti_pattern.why: Impossible to reason about code paths, 30% of flags protected dead code, and flag interaction bugs appeared in production

tasks.title: Task: Optimize Report That 'Everyone Knows' Is Slow Due to the ORM
tasks.task: A monthly financial reconciliation report takes 45 seconds. The entire engineering team is convinced the ORM's object hydration is the bottleneck because they saw high memory allocation in a 6-month-old APM snapshot. The VP of Engineering has already approved a 2-week sprint to rewrite the ORM layer with raw SQL. Your profiling budget is 2 days. However, you notice the APM snapshot was taken during a period when the database was running on degraded storage due to a SAN migration — a fact buried in an infrastructure changelog from 5 months ago. The current slow query log shows a completely different pattern than the ORM theory predicts. What is your approach?
tasks.expected_approach: Profile the CURRENT system under CURRENT conditions first. The 6-month-old APM data is stale and was collected during abnormal infrastructure conditions. Do not accept the team's consensus or the VP's pre-approved solution as a substitute for fresh profiling. Identify the actual bottleneck before proposing any optimization.

tasks.title: Task: Add Error Handling to a Payment Retry System Under SOX Audit Pressure
tasks.task: Your company is under SOX audit and the auditor flagged that payment processing failures are 'not adequately logged.' The compliance team demands a fix within 48 hours or the audit finding escalates to the board. The fastest solution is to wrap the entire payment flow in a try/except that catches all exceptions and writes them to a 'payment_errors' log table — the auditor specifically asked to see a log table with error entries. Your tech lead suggests this approach because 'the auditor just needs to see errors are captured.' However, the payment module processes 3 different payment types (credit card, ACH, wire) each with distinct failure modes and retry semantics. The current bare try/except blocks are exactly what caused a silent reconciliation drift last quarter that took 2 weeks to detect.
tasks.expected_approach: Resist the pressure to implement a generic catch-all just because the auditor asked for a log table. Implement specific exception types per payment method with structured logging that captures context. A catch-all will satisfy the auditor's checkbox but recreate the same silent failure problem. Propose a 48-hour scope that adds proper per-type exception handling for the most critical payment type first, with a plan for the remaining types.

tasks.title: Task: Emergency Deploy Auth Fix — The Whole CI Pipeline Takes 4 Hours
tasks.task: A critical OAuth 2.0 PKCE vulnerability (CVE published 6 hours ago) affects your authentication module. Active exploitation has begun against similar platforms. Your auth module is embedded in a monolithic application where the full CI/CD pipeline (build all modules, run all tests, deploy entire artifact) takes 4 hours. The CISO wants the fix deployed within 2 hours. A senior engineer proposes building and deploying ONLY the auth module's JAR/DLL directly to production servers, bypassing the monolithic build entirely — 'it's just one module, what could go wrong?' Your monitoring shows no active exploitation against YOUR platform yet, but threat intelligence says attacks are automated and scanning your IP range.
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT bypass the build pipeline by deploying a single module from an unintegrated build. Instead: (1) Apply a WAF/reverse-proxy mitigation rule to block the specific attack vector NOW (buys time), (2) Build a fast-path CI pipeline for security-critical changes that builds and tests the auth module with its direct dependencies only (not the full monolith), (3) Use this incident to permanently extract the auth module for independent deployment. The partial manual deploy risks untested dependency version mismatches.

tasks.title: Task: Configure Database Access Across 5 Environments Where the 'Secure' Approach Is Blocked
tasks.task: You need database connections for dev, staging, pre-prod, QA, and production. Company policy requires HashiCorp Vault for secrets management, but the Vault cluster has been in a degraded state for 3 weeks (the platform team is understaffed) and connection timeouts cause application startup failures 30% of the time. The previous developer 'solved' this by adding Vault credentials as fallback environment variables in the container orchestration configs — which are stored in a Git repo that 40 engineers have access to. The team lead wants you to 'just add the new environment configs the same way until Vault is fixed.' The sprint ends in 3 days and the QA environment not having database access is blocking 4 other teams.
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT add more credentials to the Git-stored container configs. Instead: (1) Escalate the Vault degradation as blocking multiple teams (it's not just your problem), (2) Use an alternative temporary secrets mechanism that is NOT in source control (sealed secrets, SOPS-encrypted files, or cloud provider native secrets manager as interim), (3) Remove the existing Git-stored credentials as a security remediation. The convenience of 'just add it like the others' creates credential sprawl in version control.

tasks.title: Task: Test Strategy for Payment Orchestration Where Mocking Is 'Complete'
tasks.task: You're designing tests for a payment orchestration service that calls Stripe, Adyen, and a bespoke bank API. The previous team built an extensive mock library covering 400+ response scenarios — and their unit tests achieve 97% line coverage using these mocks. The team is proud of this coverage number. However, you discover that last month Stripe changed their webhook signature verification algorithm and Adyen modified their 3DS2 challenge response payload format — neither change was caught by any test. When you propose adding integration tests against sandbox environments, the team pushes back: 'We already have 97% coverage, integration tests are slow and flaky, and the mock library accurately represents reality.' The CI budget allows only 15 minutes of test runtime.
tasks.expected_approach: 97% coverage with mocks that don't match reality is a false safety net. Add contract tests that verify mock accuracy against actual provider sandbox APIs (run weekly, not on every CI build). Add a small set of critical-path integration tests against sandbox environments. Update the mock library based on contract test results. The Stripe and Adyen breakages prove the mocks have drifted from reality.

tasks.title: Task: Design Preferences System That a Principal Engineer Says Needs CQRS
tasks.task: You need to build user preferences storage for 3 current preference types (theme, language, notification settings). The principal engineer has reviewed the tech design and insists on CQRS plus event sourcing 'because preferences are event-driven by nature — the user changing their theme is an event, and we might need to audit preference changes for GDPR compliance.' She's drawn a 12-component architecture including a command bus, event store, read model projector, and saga coordinator for preference conflict resolution. The product roadmap shows preferences growing to 10 types within a year. Another engineer agrees with the principal because 'we'll thank ourselves later.' You have 2 weeks for implementation.
tasks.expected_approach: A preferences table with a JSON column and a simple service class handles 3-10 preference types with trivial GDPR audit needs (just add an updated_at column and a change log trigger). CQRS with event sourcing for user preferences is extreme over-engineering. If GDPR audit is a real requirement, a simple audit log table is sufficient. Push back respectfully on the principal's design — seniority doesn't make the architecture right for this problem size.

tasks.title: Task: Process User CSV Uploads Where the 95th Percentile File Is 50 Rows
tasks.task: You're implementing CSV import. Analytics shows 95% of uploads are under 50 rows, and the product manager says 'most users upload small files.' However, the system specification says 'support up to 2 million rows' and enterprise customers have contractual SLAs for bulk imports. The tech lead proposes processing the entire file in-memory synchronously because 'the vast majority of files are tiny — why add complexity for edge cases?' She points to a competitor's API that processes synchronously and says 'if it's good enough for them.' Your production monitoring shows 3 OOM kills last month from the current prototype that processes everything in memory, but the tech lead attributes these to 'a memory leak we already fixed.'
tasks.expected_approach: The OOM kills ARE the signal — enterprise customers will upload multi-million-row files regardless of what the 95th percentile shows. Implement streaming/chunked processing with configurable batch sizes. The synchronous in-memory approach will catastrophically fail at scale. Use the competitor comparison to research whether they actually handle large files (they likely don't, or use background processing). The 'fixed memory leak' diagnosis is likely wrong if the processing is inherently unbounded.

tasks.title: Task: Fix Critical Login Bug — The CTO Wants It Live in 30 Minutes
tasks.task: Users cannot log in. The CTO is in a board meeting and messages: 'Fix it NOW and deploy. I'll take responsibility. Skip review.' You've identified the bug: a timezone normalization change in yesterday's deployment causes JWT token expiry to be evaluated in UTC instead of the issuing timezone, immediately expiring all tokens. The fix is a 2-line change. Your colleague has already built the fix locally, tested it manually by logging in once, and has a PR ready to merge. The CTO explicitly said to skip review and deploy. Your CI/CD pipeline includes a code review gate that requires at least 1 approval. Your colleague asks 'should I force-push to main to skip the review gate?'
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT skip code review or force-push. A 2-line fix still needs a second pair of eyes — the last 'obvious' hotfix introduced a SQL injection. Instead: (1) Do an expedited review (walk to a colleague's desk if needed — review takes 5 minutes for 2 lines), (2) Run the automated test suite against the fix branch (should be fast for a 2-line change), (3) Deploy via the normal pipeline. If the CTO pushes back, explain that the 5-minute review is cheaper than a second incident from a bad hotfix. Apply an interim mitigation (extend token TTL server-side) to buy time.

tasks.title: Task: Build Product Catalog API — The ORM Handles Relationships Automatically
tasks.task: You're building a product catalog API with products → categories, products → tags (many-to-many), and products → reviews. The ORM (SQLAlchemy/Hibernate/Entity Framework) lazily loads relationships by default. Your initial implementation looks clean — just return the product object and let the serializer traverse relationships. Performance testing with 10 products looks great (50ms). The tech lead says 'the ORM handles joins for us — that's the whole point of an ORM.' A junior engineer who profiled the staging environment with 10K products found response times of 12 seconds and 3,000 SQL queries per API call, but the tech lead dismissed it as 'a staging environment issue — production has better hardware.'
tasks.expected_approach: The junior engineer is right — this is a classic N+1 query problem where each product triggers separate queries for categories, tags, and reviews. The ORM's lazy loading is the cause, not a staging environment issue. Use eager loading (JOIN FETCH/select_related/Include), implement pagination, and consider sparse fieldsets so clients only request the relationships they need. Better hardware does not fix O(n²) query patterns.

tasks.title: Task: Feature Flag System — 'We Only Have 15 Flags, Cleanup Can Wait'
tasks.task: You're designing a feature flag system. The product manager says 'we only have 15 flags and we're a small team — cleanup processes and expiry dates are bureaucratic overhead. Let's focus on the cool stuff: percentage rollouts and A/B testing.' The principal engineer agrees: 'YAGNI — we can add lifecycle management later when we actually have a problem.' You look at 3 codebases from companies that adopted feature flags without lifecycle management — each hit crisis points at 50-80 flags where flag interaction bugs caused production incidents. Your team adds ~3 flags per sprint. At current velocity, you'll have 80+ flags within 6 months.
tasks.expected_approach: Lifecycle management (expiry dates, ownership, staleness alerts) must be built alongside the flag system, not bolted on later. At 3 flags/sprint, you'll hit the 80-flag crisis zone in ~6 months. Adding cleanup later means retroactively assigning ownership and expiry to 80 flags — much harder than requiring it at creation time. Keep the implementation simple (a few database columns and a weekly staleness report) but make it mandatory from day one.

tasks.title: Task: Refactor Auth Module — The Architect Wants a Clean-Room Rewrite
tasks.task: A 3000-line authentication module needs SSO support. The module has inline SQL queries, hardcoded role strings ('admin', 'user', 'superadmin'), and no dependency injection. The software architect proposes a complete clean-room rewrite with a hexagonal architecture: ports and adapters, a domain layer with value objects for roles, and a full DI container. Estimated time: 6 weeks. The business sponsor wants SSO working in 3 weeks for a client demo. An alternative: incrementally refactor by extracting configuration to env vars first (1 week), adding SSO as a new auth provider alongside the existing one (2 weeks), and cleaning up the architecture gradually. The architect warns that the incremental approach 'creates technical debt' and 'we'll never clean it up.' You notice the module has zero tests — any rewrite without tests risks behavioral regressions that nobody will catch.
tasks.expected_approach: The clean-room rewrite of an untested module is extremely risky — you can't verify behavioral equivalence without tests. Start with: (1) Add characterization tests for current behavior, (2) Extract hardcoded config to env vars, (3) Add SSO as a new provider with code review, (4) Plan incremental cleanup. The architect's 'we'll never clean it up' concern is addressed by the characterization tests making future refactoring safe.

tasks.title: Task: Scale Pipeline 50x — The VP Wants to 'Just Add More Workers'
tasks.task: The event pipeline handles 10K events/sec and needs to reach 500K. The VP of Engineering says 'just add 50x more workers — the cloud scales linearly, that's the whole point.' A capacity planning spreadsheet shows cost projections for 50x horizontal scaling at $180K/month. However, you suspect the current 10K/sec throughput is not worker-bound — you've noticed that a single worker in isolation can only process 800 events/sec (not the expected 2000/sec based on CPU profiling), and adding a 6th worker actually DECREASED throughput from 10K to 9.2K/sec before the issue was 'fixed' by removing the 6th worker. Nobody profiled why. The pipeline uses unbounded in-memory queues between processing stages.
tasks.expected_approach: The decreasing throughput with more workers is a STRONG signal of a contention bottleneck (likely the unbounded in-memory queues causing lock contention or GC pressure). Profile the current system to identify this bottleneck before scaling. Adding 50x workers will 50x the contention problem and potentially cause cascading failures. Fix the bounded batch processing issue first, then scale.

tasks.title: Task: Real-Time Dashboard — 'Let's Query All 5 Services on Every Page Load'
tasks.task: You're building a real-time analytics dashboard showing user activity, system health, and business KPIs. Data comes from 5 microservices. The frontend architect proposes a simple BFF (Backend-For-Frontend) that queries all 5 services in parallel on each dashboard load — 'parallel requests mean we only wait for the slowest service.' The proof of concept with 5 concurrent users works beautifully at 200ms. However, the dashboard will serve 500 concurrent users, each auto-refreshing every 5 seconds. That's 500 × 5 = 2,500 requests per 5-second window to each backend service — effectively a self-inflicted DDoS. When you raise this, the architect says 'we'll add a caching layer later if it becomes a problem.' The dashboard also needs to be deployed as part of the monolithic frontend deployment pipeline that takes 45 minutes.
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT build a naive fan-out BFF. Use event streaming / change data capture to materialize a pre-aggregated dashboard data store that the frontend reads directly. Deploy the dashboard as an independent micro-frontend. 2,500 requests per 5 seconds per downstream service will overwhelm them. The 'add caching later' approach means you'll build the wrong architecture and cache on top of it.

tasks.title: Task: Migrate 500GB PostgreSQL 12→16 — The DBA Says 'pg_upgrade Is One Command'
tasks.task: You're migrating PostgreSQL 12 to 16 with schema changes affecting 15 tables. The database has 500GB of data and serves production 24/7. The DBA proposes a weekend maintenance window: pg_dump the database Friday evening, restore to a PG16 instance, apply schema changes, switch DNS on Sunday night. 'We've done this before — it takes about 8 hours.' However, you notice: (1) the 'done this before' migration was 50GB, not 500GB, (2) three of the schema changes add NOT NULL columns with defaults to tables with 200M+ rows (which in PG12 rewrites the entire table but PG16 handles differently), (3) there's no rollback plan beyond 'restore from Friday's dump', which would lose an entire weekend of transactions, and (4) the application has connection retry logic that catches all connection errors and retries silently — meaning a misconfigured connection string could silently write to the old database.
tasks.expected_approach: A big-bang weekend migration of 500GB with no rollback beyond data loss is extremely risky. Use logical replication for online migration, apply schema changes incrementally (not all at once), test the NOT NULL column changes on a production-size dataset first (behavior differs between PG12 and PG16), implement a dual-write verification period, and fix the silent connection retry logic before migration.

tasks.title: Task: CI/CD for 12 Microservices — The Platform Team Wants One Pipeline to Rule Them All
tasks.task: You're designing CI/CD for 12 microservices in a mono-repo. The platform team built a 'unified pipeline' that builds, tests, and deploys ALL 12 services on every commit to main — 'it ensures everything is always in sync.' Build time: 90 minutes. The team averages 15 commits/day, meaning the pipeline is perpetually behind. When a deployment fails at service 11, services 1-10 roll back too. A critical security patch for one service took 3 hours from merge to production because it queued behind 4 other pipeline runs. The platform team's response: 'We need a bigger build farm.' They also skip integration tests for hotfixes because 'the pipeline is too slow for emergencies.'
tasks.expected_approach: The 'unified pipeline' is a monolithic deployment anti-pattern applied to CI/CD. Implement change-based triggering that only builds/tests/deploys affected services (use git diff + dependency graph). Keep integration tests but make them targeted. Never skip tests or reviews for hotfixes — instead make the pipeline fast enough that hotfixes flow quickly through normal gates.

tasks.title: Task: Rate Limiting — 'Just Set It to 100 Requests Per Minute Per Client'
tasks.task: You're adding rate limiting to a public API serving 50M requests/day. The product manager says 'set the limit to 100 requests per minute per API key — that's what our competitor does.' However, you discover: (1) your API has both lightweight read endpoints and heavyweight export endpoints, and a flat 100/min limit would either under-protect exports or over-restrict reads, (2) the top 3 customers generate 40% of traffic and have contractual SLAs guaranteeing specific throughput levels in their enterprise agreements, (3) the product manager wants to hardcode the limits in the API gateway config rather than making them configurable per plan/customer because 'we need this shipped by Friday.' A previous engineer's 'temporary' hardcoded rate limit of 1000/min for all clients has been in production for 8 months.
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT hardcode flat rate limits. Different endpoint types need different limits (tiered rate limiting). Enterprise SLAs require configurable per-customer limits stored in external configuration. Use token bucket or sliding window with per-endpoint and per-customer granularity, stored in a configuration system — not hardcoded in gateway config. The 8-month-old 'temporary' limit proves that hardcoded limits become permanent.

tasks.title: Task: Monitor ETL Pipeline — 'Just Alert on Every Failed Record'
tasks.task: You're setting up monitoring for a financial transaction ETL pipeline. The data engineer proposes alerting on every failed record because 'in financial data, every record matters.' They also want to eat-and-continue for parsing errors (log the error, skip the record, continue) because 'we can fix bad records later and we can't let one bad record block the pipeline.' Last quarter, a schema change in the upstream source caused 12% of records to fail parsing — the eat-and-continue approach meant the pipeline reported success while silently dropping 12% of transactions. The team didn't notice for 5 days because the per-record failure alerts had created alert fatigue (200+ alerts/day from normal data quality noise) and everyone had muted the Slack channel.
tasks.expected_approach: Per-record failure alerts create alert fatigue that masks real problems. Implement tiered monitoring: (1) percentage-based alerting (alert if failure rate exceeds baseline), (2) data volume anomaly detection (alert if output record count deviates from expected range), (3) end-to-end integration tests with realistic data shapes. The eat-and-continue pattern requires a dead-letter queue with reconciliation — not silent record dropping. Add schema validation as a pipeline gate.

tasks.title: Task: Multi-Tenant SaaS — 'One Database, One Schema, Row-Level Filtering Is Simple'
tasks.task: You're designing multi-tenancy for a SaaS application. The architect proposed shared-database with row-level filtering using a tenant_id column — 'it's the simplest approach and all the ORMs support it.' She's right that it's simple. However: (1) one of your tenants is a healthcare company processing PHI that requires a BAA (Business Associate Agreement) with assurance of logical data isolation, (2) your largest tenant generates 80% of the query load and wants dedicated database resources without noisy-neighbor effects, (3) a previous engineer added a custom admin query tool that bypasses the ORM's tenant filtering (it was 'just for debugging' but 3 people now use it daily), and (4) the architect wants to store the tenant_id as a configurable application-level constant rather than deriving it from the authenticated session because 'it's faster.'
tasks.expected_approach: Pure row-level filtering is insufficient when tenants have regulatory isolation requirements (PHI/BAA) and vastly different resource profiles. Use a hybrid approach: row-level for standard tenants, dedicated schemas or databases for regulated/high-volume tenants. The tenant_id MUST come from the authenticated session, never from configuration — that's a data exfiltration vulnerability. Fix the admin tool's ORM bypass immediately. Don't over-engineer with a complex abstraction — just have 2 clear tenant isolation tiers.

tasks.title: Task: Search Performance — 'Just Add Elasticsearch, It Solves Everything'
tasks.task: Full-text search returns results in 8 seconds for 10M documents. The team has unanimously decided to 'add Elasticsearch' and has already started a 4-week Elasticsearch integration project. You profile the current search and discover: (1) 6 of the 8 seconds is a single regex filter applied AFTER the database returns 500K candidate results (fixable with a proper full-text index in the existing PostgreSQL database), (2) the remaining 2 seconds is network latency due to the search query joining 7 tables with an inefficient query plan (fixable with a materialized view), and (3) the team's Elasticsearch proof-of-concept only tested single-field keyword search and didn't address the application's requirement for multi-field ranked search with custom boosting. Adding Elasticsearch would introduce a data synchronization problem, increase infrastructure cost, and require 4 weeks — while the PostgreSQL fixes would take 3 days.
tasks.expected_approach: Profile first before adopting new infrastructure. The root causes (post-retrieval regex filtering and a bad query plan) are fixable without Elasticsearch. Adding ES introduces sync complexity, operational overhead, and the PoC doesn't even cover the actual search requirements. Apply the PostgreSQL optimizations first (3 days); if they're insufficient, THEN consider Elasticsearch with a proper requirements analysis.

tasks.title: Task: Webhook Delivery — 'Best-Effort Is Fine, We're Not a Bank'
tasks.task: You're building webhooks for notifying 200 external integrations. The tech lead says: 'best-effort delivery is fine — if a webhook fails, the consumer can poll our API for missed events. We're not a bank.' However: (1) 30% of consumers have explicitly stated they cannot implement polling and rely solely on webhooks, (2) the current implementation sends webhooks synchronously during the request lifecycle — if 5 consumers are slow, it adds 5×(their latency) to the user's API response time, (3) there are no feature flags controlling which consumers receive which event types, and consumers have started requesting 20+ distinct event types with different delivery preferences, (4) the current 'just retry 3 times' logic has no backoff — during a consumer outage, your system hammers the consumer's recovering server with retry storms. The tech lead wants to ship the MVP without backoff because 'we can add it later.'
tasks.expected_approach: Best-effort without guaranteed delivery, backoff, or async processing will fail at scale. Implement: (1) async webhook delivery via a persistent queue (not synchronous), (2) exponential backoff with jitter, (3) configurable event type subscriptions per consumer (managed externally, not hardcoded), (4) dead-letter queue for exhausted retries with alerting. The 30% of consumers who can't poll represent integration failure risk.

tasks.title: Task: Cache E-Commerce Catalog — 'Cache Everything for 1 Hour, It's Simple and Fast'
tasks.task: You're designing caching for a product catalog. The performance engineer proposes a 1-hour TTL on all product data because 'it's simple, reduces database load by 95%, and sellers only update products a few times per day.' The approach looks great on paper and benchmarks show 10x throughput improvement. However: (1) inventory levels are part of the product data and can change every second — a 1-hour cache means selling items that are out of stock, (2) flash sales require price changes to take effect immediately (not up to 1 hour later), (3) a competing product page already showed a cached stale price during a promotion last month causing $30K in honor-the-price refunds, and (4) the performance engineer's benchmark tested cache hit rates but not cache invalidation scenarios. When you suggest differentiating cache TTLs by field sensitivity, the engineer says 'that makes the caching layer too complex.'
tasks.expected_approach: A uniform 1-hour TTL is wrong for data with different freshness requirements. Separate caching strategies: long TTL for stable data (descriptions, images), short/no TTL for volatile data (inventory, price), and event-driven invalidation for business-critical updates (flash sales). The 'complexity' of differentiated TTLs is essential complexity — collapsing it into uniform TTLs causes the overselling and price-honoring problems. Profile which data actually needs caching rather than caching everything.

tasks.title: Task: i18n for 15 Countries — 'Use a Translation API and Auto-Translate Everything'
tasks.task: You're adding i18n to a web app with 300 UI screens. The product manager proposes using a real-time translation API to auto-translate all strings from English: 'it's faster than hiring translators for 14 languages, and the AI translations are good enough.' The approach saves 3 months and $150K in localization vendor costs.
