However: (1) your app includes legal terms (privacy policy, terms of service, cookie consent) where machine translation creates liability in multiple jurisdictions, (2) the RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew) need layout mirroring that auto-translation doesn't address, (3) the auto-translate approach hardcodes English as the source of truth, making it impossible for local teams to maintain their language independently, and (4) locale-specific formatting (date, currency, number separators) isn't addressed by string translation at all. The PM says 'we can fix legal and RTL issues after launch.'
tasks.expected_approach: Use a proper i18n library with externalized resource bundles per locale. Machine translation can accelerate the initial translation but cannot be the production approach for legal content or RTL layouts. Keep the i18n framework simple (standard library, string bundles), don't hardcode English as the source language, and handle RTL as a core layout requirement from the start — not a post-launch fix. Legal translations require human review.

tasks.title: Task: Financial Audit Logging — 'Append to the Same Table as Application Logs'
tasks.task: You're building audit logging for a financial application. The DBA proposes using the existing application log table: 'It already captures who, what, when, and which resource. Adding before/after values is just 2 more columns. Why build a separate system?' The application log table is convenient and already has 18 months of data. However: (1) application logs are routinely purged after 90 days per the log retention policy — audit trails have a 7-year regulatory retention requirement, (2) the application log table uses auto-increment IDs with gaps (from batch deletions during cleanup) — gaps in audit trail sequence numbers are a red flag for compliance auditors, (3) developers currently have DELETE access to the application log table for operational troubleshooting, which means the audit trail is not tamper-evident, and (4) the application logs generate 50M rows/day versus an expected 200K audit events/day, meaning audit queries would scan a table 250x larger than necessary. The DBA says 'we can add an index and a retention filter.'
tasks.expected_approach: Audit logs MUST be separate from application logs. Different retention requirements, different access controls (no DELETE for anyone on audit tables), tamper-evident storage (append-only with cryptographic chaining), and different query patterns. An index on the application log table doesn't solve retention, tampering, or the compliance audit concerns about sequence gaps. Build a separate audit event store.

tasks.title: Task: Graceful Degradation — 'Just Return the Last Known Good Price'
tasks.task: You're implementing fallback pricing for a ride-sharing app when the real-time demand model is unavailable. The data science team says: 'just cache the last known surge multiplier and return that when the model is down.' This seems reasonable — it's recent data and users won't notice small deviations. However: (1) the last known multiplier could be a 3.5x surge from a concert that ended 2 hours ago, applied to a user in a quiet residential area, (2) during a recent 30-minute model outage, the 'last known good' price was from a modeling A/B test group that applied experimental aggressive pricing — meaning 50% of users got prices 40% higher than the control group, and (3) no one profiled what actual failure modes look like — the model doesn't fail cleanly; it sometimes returns obviously wrong values (negative multipliers, 100x surges) that the current system passes through because there's no output validation. The team says 'adding validation is premature optimization of the error path.'
tasks.expected_approach: Implement fallback pricing tiers based on time-of-day and zone averages (not last-known-good which may be stale or from an A/B experiment). Add output validation on the demand model's responses to catch obviously wrong values BEFORE they reach users — this isn't premature optimization, it's input validation at a system boundary. Profile actual failure modes (partial failures, stale data, experimental data) and design fallbacks for each.

tasks.title: Task: Data Export — 'Generate the File Synchronously and Stream It Down'
tasks.task: You're building GDPR data portability export. The frontend architect proposes: 'just have the API generate the file and stream it as the HTTP response — the user clicks Export, waits a few seconds, and the browser downloads it. Simple.' For 90% of users (under 10K records), this works fine. However: (1) 3% of users have 1M+ records and their export takes 15 minutes — but the load balancer's request timeout is 60 seconds, (2) during the last product demo, the CEO tried to export their own massive test account and got a browser timeout error in front of 200 customers, (3) the synchronous export also holds a database read transaction open for the entire duration, which blocked a scheduled migration last weekend, and (4) GDPR requires the export to include data from 6 different services (auth, billing, activity, preferences, support tickets, analytics), meaning 6 cross-service calls during one synchronous request. The architect says 'we can just increase the load balancer timeout.'
tasks.expected_approach: Synchronous generation fails for large exports and holds resources (DB transactions, HTTP connections) open for minutes. Use async processing: (1) Accept export request, return job ID, (2) Generate in background with chunked processing and progress tracking, (3) Notify user on completion, (4) Provide download link with expiry. Handle partial failures from the 6 cross-service calls individually. Increasing the load balancer timeout is a bandaid that creates cascading resource exhaustion.

tasks.title: Task: REST-to-GraphQL Migration — 'Replace All 40 Endpoints in One Release'
tasks.task: You're migrating a REST API with 40 endpoints to GraphQL. 100 client applications integrate with the API. The engineering manager says: 'Let's set a migration deadline — GraphQL goes live on June 1st, REST is deprecated. Clients have 60 days notice.' The GraphQL implementation uses auto-generated resolvers from the database schema. The team is excited because 'clients can query exactly what they need.' However: (1) the auto-generated resolvers resolve each relationship with a separate database query (N+1), (2) three of the 40 REST endpoints have complex business logic in controller middleware that doesn't translate to GraphQL resolvers, (3) 20 of the 100 clients are on legacy platforms that have no GraphQL library available, (4) the 'deprecation' plan has no mechanism to identify which clients have migrated and which haven't, and (5) a major enterprise client's contract guarantees REST API access for 18 months. The manager says 'we'll add DataLoader later' and 'legacy clients can use the GraphQL HTTP endpoint like a REST endpoint.'
tasks.expected_approach: Big-bang migration of 40 endpoints for 100 clients is a monolithic deployment. Implement GraphQL as an additional layer alongside REST (BFF pattern), add DataLoader upfront (not later), migrate individual endpoints incrementally, track client migration status, and respect the enterprise contract. Do not auto-generate resolvers from the database — map them to the existing business logic.

tasks.title: Task: Distributed Locking — 'Just Use Redis SETNX, It's Straightforward'
tasks.task: You're building distributed locking for 8 batch job workers. A senior engineer proposes a simple approach: 'Use Redis SETNX with a 5-minute TTL. If you get the lock, run the job. Simple and proven.' She's implemented this pattern before and it worked. However: (1) your Redis is a single-node replicated setup, and during a recent failover, the replica didn't have the lock key — two workers acquired the 'same' lock simultaneously and ran the same batch job twice (producing duplicate processing), (2) the 5-minute TTL was chosen arbitrarily, but one of the batch jobs sometimes takes 7 minutes — the lock expires mid-execution and another worker starts the same job, (3) there's no heartbeat mechanism so a dead worker's lock is held for the full 5 minutes, blocking other healthy workers, and (4) the error handling catches Redis connection errors with a bare except and proceeds without the lock. The senior engineer says 'the failover issue was a one-time thing' and 'we can increase the TTL to 30 minutes.'
tasks.expected_approach: Simple SETNX on a single Redis node does NOT provide distributed lock safety guarantees during failover. Use a proven distributed lock algorithm (Redlock with multiple independent Redis instances, or ZooKeeper/etcd), implement lock renewal heartbeat (not an arbitrary TTL), externalize TTL configuration, and NEVER proceed without the lock on Redis connection errors — that defeats the entire purpose of locking.

tasks.title: Task: Content Pipeline — 'Transcode All Videos When They're Uploaded'
tasks.task: You're building a content delivery pipeline for a media platform. The media team proposes transcoding all uploaded video into 6 quality tiers (360p through 4K) immediately upon upload — 'YouTube does this, it gives the best user experience.' Each video averages 200MB, daily uploads average 500 videos, and transcoding takes ~10 minutes per video per tier (60 minutes total for all 6 tiers). The entire platform runs as a monolithic deployment. However: (1) 60% of videos are viewed only in 360p and 720p — the 1080p/4K transcodes are never accessed, (2) during a product launch featuring 50 simultaneous video uploads, the transcoding queue backed up to 9 hours because all 300 transcode jobs (50 × 6 tiers) competed for the same resource pool with no priority or throttling, (3) the monolithic deployment means a content pipeline fix requires redeploying the entire platform. The media team says 'storage is cheap and transcoding everything upfront is simpler than on-demand.'
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT transcode all 6 tiers eagerly. Use on-demand transcoding for rarely-accessed tiers (1440p, 4K), eager transcoding for popular tiers (360p, 720p), bounded concurrent transcoding with priority queues, and deploy the content pipeline as an independent service. The 9-hour backup during launch proves unbounded batch processing fails under load spikes.

tasks.title: Task: Permission System — 'Let's Build a Policy Engine with a Custom DSL'
tasks.task: You're designing a role-based access control system. The security architect proposes a full attribute-based access control (ABAC) system with a custom policy DSL because 'RBAC is too rigid — we need policies like: allow if user.department == resource.department AND user.clearance >= resource.classification AND time.hour is between 9 and 17.' The architecture includes a custom policy language parser, a policy evaluation engine, and a policy admin UI. Estimated build time: 8 weeks. Currently the application has: 4 roles (admin, manager, editor, viewer), 3 resource types, and no plans to add more for 12 months. The security architect points to Google Zanzibar as inspiration. When you suggest a simpler RBAC-first approach with attribute checks only where needed, she says 'that's how we accumulate permission debt.'
tasks.expected_approach: Building a custom ABAC policy engine with DSL for 4 roles and 3 resource types is extreme over-engineering. Google Zanzibar was built for billions of objects — not a system with 12 permission combinations. Start with standard RBAC (a proven library, not custom), externalize permission definitions (not hardcoded), and add attribute checks at specific decision points only where RBAC is genuinely insufficient. Consider adopting OPA/Cedar if ABAC becomes necessary — don't build a custom DSL.

tasks.title: Task: Session-to-JWT Migration — 'Do It All At Once During the Next Maintenance Window'
tasks.task: You're migrating from session-based to JWT-based auth for 50K concurrent users. The plan: during the monthly 2-hour maintenance window, deploy JWT support, invalidate all sessions, force all users to re-login, and decommission the session store. The engineering manager says: 'Clean cutover. No messy dual-auth code. Users log in once and they're on JWTs forever.' QA tested the JWT implementation thoroughly in staging. However: (1) the 2-hour window may not be long enough if issues arise — the session store decommission is irreversible, (2) 15% of users are on mobile apps that cache auth state and won't see the re-login prompt until the next app restart (potentially days later), (3) the JWT expiry/refresh logic has never been tested under 50K concurrent load, only 500 in staging, (4) there's no way to flag which users have already migrated to JWT vs which are still session-based, and (5) the feature flag for the JWT feature doesn't have an expiry date — the last 'temporary' dual-auth feature flag is still in the codebase from 2 years ago.
tasks.expected_approach: A big-bang session→JWT cutover is extremely risky for 50K users. Use dual-auth with a feature flag (WITH an expiry date): (1) New logins get JWTs, existing sessions remain valid, (2) Gradually migrate users as sessions expire, (3) Monitor JWT under increasing real load, (4) Set feature flag expiry for session auth removal in 30 days, (5) Only decommission session store after migration metrics confirm 99.9% of users are on JWT.

tasks.title: Task: Email Templates — 'Just Use String Interpolation in the Code'
tasks.task: You're implementing transactional emails (order confirmation, password reset, etc.). The developer's initial implementation uses inline HTML template strings in the code with f-string interpolation for dynamic values. They argue: 'templates are just strings — why do we need a template engine? String interpolation is simpler and we can see the template right next to the code.' For A/B testing subject lines, they propose: 'just add an if/else in the send_email() function with the variant.' The marketing team has already asked for 5 more email types this quarter, and A/B test results are tracked in a shared spreadsheet that someone updates manually after each test concludes. When you suggest a template engine with externalized templates, the developer says 'that's over-engineering for email.'
tasks.expected_approach: Inline HTML templates with f-strings create unmaintainable coupling between code and content, prevent non-developers from editing templates, and expose the application to template injection risks with user-provided values. Use a template engine (Jinja2, Handlebars) with externalized template files. For A/B testing, store variants with lifecycle management (winner selection, automatic test conclusion) — not a manual spreadsheet. The growing template demand (5 more this quarter) will make the inline approach collapse quickly.

tasks.title: Task: Message Retry Strategy — 'Retry Forever Until It Works'
tasks.task: You're designing retry logic for an order fulfillment pipeline. The product owner says: 'orders are sacred — we should retry FOREVER until the message is processed. We can't lose orders.' The initial implementation retries every 5 seconds indefinitely. In staging, this looks fine. However: (1) during a 4-hour partner API outage last quarter, the unbounded retry created a 2.8-million message queue backlog that took 18 hours to drain after the API recovered, consuming all available workers, (2) the constant retry rate meant that when the partner API came back up, your system hit it with 50K requests in the first minute — triggering their rate limiter and extending the outage, (3) some messages fail due to data validation errors (not transient network errors) and will NEVER succeed no matter how many times they're retried, (4) the product owner has started treating the retry queue as a 'self-healing' system and no longer monitors fulfillment failures. The product owner says 'better to be slow than lose orders.'
tasks.expected_approach: Infinite retries with fixed intervals cause queue backlogs and downstream DDoS during outages. Implement: (1) Bounded retries with exponential backoff and jitter, (2) Distinguish transient errors (retry with backoff) from permanent errors (send to DLQ immediately), (3) Circuit breaker to stop retries during extended outages, (4) DLQ with monitoring and alerting for manual investigation. 'Retry forever' creates the illusion of reliability while masking real failures.

tasks.title: Task: API Versioning — 'Put the Version in Every Model, Method, and DTO'
tasks.task: You need API versioning for 500+ third-party integrations. The tech lead proposes: 'namespace everything by version — separate controller classes, separate request/response DTOs, separate service layer methods. v1 and v2 are completely independent code paths.' This ensures 'v1 changes can never accidentally affect v2.' In the proof of concept, v1 and v2 of one endpoint look clean. However: (1) the API has 40 endpoints, and duplicating everything creates 80 separate code paths with only ~10% actual differences, (2) a critical security fix needs to be applied to BOTH versions, but the duplicated code means patching it in 2 places (and the developer who patched v2 forgot to patch v1 — discovered 2 weeks later), (3) adding v3 would triple the maintenance burden, and (4) the deprecation plan says 'maintain both versions indefinitely' with no sunset dates because 'we don't want to break integrations.' Integration partners are given no incentive or tooling to migrate.
tasks.expected_approach: Full code duplication per version creates unmaintainable divergence. Use URL path versioning with a shared implementation layer and version-specific adapters (request/response transformation) for the ~10% that differs. Limit concurrent supported versions to 2. Establish a deprecation policy with sunset dates and migration tooling. Security fixes must apply to the shared layer, not be duplicated across version forks.

tasks.title: Task: Activity Feed — 'Fan-Out-On-Write for Everyone, Twitter Does It'
tasks.task: You're building an activity feed for 2M users with average 150 follows. The architect proposes fan-out-on-write for all users: 'when a user posts, push the activity to all followers' feeds immediately. Twitter pioneered this.' The prototype with 1K test users is fast (50ms post-to-feed). However: (1) 500 'power users' have 50K-200K followers each, meaning a single post fans out to 200K feed writes taking 4 seconds, (2) during peak activity, 20 power users posting within the same minute generates 4M write operations — saturating the database write capacity, (3) the performance test only measured small-follower-count users and declared 'fan-out-on-write is fast', (4) feed entries for inactive users (40% of the userbase) consume storage but are never read. The architect says 'we'll add a celebrity exception later' and 'we should pre-allocate based on worst case.'
tasks.expected_approach: Hybrid approach: fan-out-on-write for normal users (bounded write operations), fan-out-on-read for power users with >N followers (avoiding the 200K write storm). Profile actual posting and reading patterns before deciding the threshold N. Use bounded page sizes for feed reads. Don't write to inactive users' feeds. The celebrity exception IS a core architectural decision, not a 'later' optimization.

tasks.title: Task: Data Archival — 'Just Move Everything Older Than 2 Years in a Single Batch Job'
tasks.task: You're building data archival for 50TB database. The DBA proposes a Saturday night batch job: 'DELETE FROM main_table WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 2 YEARS; we'll run SELECT INTO cold_storage first.' Simple and complete. The DBA has done this before on a 500GB database. However: (1) the DELETE on 50TB will generate massive WAL/redo logs potentially filling the disk, (2) the long-running DELETE holds row-level locks that will block production reads on referenced rows, (3) the 'SELECT INTO cold_storage' for 30TB of archival data would run for ~6 hours — well beyond the maintenance window, (4) if the job fails at 80%, the partial state requires understanding which records were archived and which weren't (there's no checkpoint mechanism), (5) regulatory compliance requires that archived records remain queryable — the cold storage doesn't have the same indexes or query interface. The DBA says 'we'll increase the maintenance window to 12 hours.'
tasks.expected_approach: A single massive batch DELETE/INSERT is catastrophic at 50TB scale. Use: (1) Chunked archival with configurable batch sizes (e.g., 10K rows per transaction), (2) Checkpointing so failures can resume from where they stopped, (3) Streaming to cold storage rather than bulk SELECT INTO, (4) Verify queryability requirements in cold storage BEFORE starting archival, (5) Run continuously in small batches rather than as a big-bang weekend job.

tasks.title: Task: Health Checks — 'Deep Health Check All Downstream Dependencies Every 10 Seconds'
tasks.task: You're designing health checks for 25 microservices. The SRE proposes: 'every service's health check should verify all its downstream dependencies — if your database, cache, or any downstream API is unhealthy, YOU are unhealthy.' The deep health check queries 3 databases, 2 cache clusters, and 4 downstream APIs on every invocation. The health check runs every 10 seconds from 3 regions. That's 25 services × 9 dependency checks × 3 regions × 6 times/minute = 4,050 synthetic transactions per minute just from health checks. During a recent database maintenance window, ALL 25 services reported unhealthy simultaneously, triggering the orchestrator to restart all of them — which caused a thundering herd that overwhelmed the recovering database. The SRE says 'we'll add a grace period before restarts.'
tasks.expected_approach: Deep health checks on every dependency create cascading failure risk. Implement tiered checks: (1) Liveness = process is running and can accept requests (no dependency checks), (2) Readiness = can serve traffic (check only direct critical dependencies), (3) Deep health = full dependency check (run infrequently, for dashboards only, NOT for orchestrator decisions). Circuit-break failing dependency checks to prevent thundering herds. Batch dependency checks rather than per-service individual queries.

tasks.title: Task: Config Management — 'Build a Custom Config Service With a Web UI'
tasks.task: You're building centralized config for 20 services. The platform team proposes building a custom config service with a React admin UI, a REST API for config CRUD, a PostgreSQL backend, a change notification system via webhooks, and an audit log. Estimated build: 10 weeks. The team has started designing the database schema. However: (1) Consul, etcd, and AWS AppConfig already do exactly this, (2) the custom service would itself need configuration (database connection, webhook endpoints) — creating a chicken-and-egg bootstrap problem, (3) the custom service becomes a critical SPOF — if it goes down, no service can read configuration, (4) the 10-week timeline means 20 services will continue hardcoding config for 10 more weeks. A team member suggests Consul but is overruled because 'we want full control over the UI and didn't like Consul's KV store interface.'
tasks.expected_approach: Do NOT build a custom config service when battle-tested solutions (Consul, etcd, AWS AppConfig) exist. The 10-week build creates a new SPOF, delays config externalization for all 20 services, and the resulting custom service needs more maintenance than the libraries it replaces. Adopt Consul/etcd, spend the 10 weeks migrating services to use it instead of building infrastructure from scratch. If the UI matters, build a thin UI wrapper on top of the existing tool.

tasks.title: Task: E2E Testing Framework — 'Record All 50 User Flows as Selenium Scripts'
tasks.task: You're setting up E2E testing for 50 user flows. The QA lead proposes: 'record all 50 flows using Selenium IDE, export to scripts, and run them in CI.' She's already recorded 15 flows and they pass locally. However: (1) recorded tests use absolute selectors (XPATH with exact positions, auto-generated IDs) that break on any UI change — the 15 recorded tests already broke twice during a CSS refactoring sprint, (2) all 50 tests run sequentially with no parallelization (estimated CI time: 90 minutes), (3) the tests have no data isolation — test 23 depends on data created by test 7, meaning a failure in test 7 cascades to 16 downstream tests, (4) the QA lead wants to record tests first and 'optimize later', (5) the CI environment has no GPU-accelerated rendering, and 12 of the tests check canvas-based chart rendering that behaves differently without GPU. The QA lead says 'recorded tests are faster to create than hand-written ones.'
tasks.expected_approach: Select test scenarios by business risk priority (not 'record all 50'). Write tests with stable selectors (data-testid attributes), implement parallel execution with data isolation per test, and start with the 10 highest-risk flows that can run reliably in CI within 15 minutes. Recorded tests create brittle, unparallelizable, slow tests. Address rendering differences (GPU/no-GPU) upfront, not after building 50 tests that fail in CI.

tasks.title: Task: Shard a 2TB Database — 'Shard by User ID Hash, It Distributes Evenly'
tasks.task: You're sharding a 2TB user database with 50K reads/sec and 5K writes/sec. The architect proposes hash-based sharding on user_id: 'perfect distribution, simple routing, no hotspots.' The proof of concept with synthetic data shows even distribution. However: (1) 30% of the read queries are cross-user aggregations (leaderboards, analytics dashboards, admin search-by-email) that would scatter across all shards, (2) the top 1% of users generate 25% of write traffic (power users) — hash distribution means you can't colocate related data, (3) the application has 15 JOIN queries that assume user and related tables (orders, preferences, activity) are in the same database, (4) the architect proposes running all cross-shard queries against a read replica that aggregates from all shards — but hasn't addressed the replication lag implications for the real-time leaderboard, (5) nobody profiled whether the 2TB database actually NEEDS sharding — the current performance issues appeared 3 months ago during a period when the connection pooler was misconfigured. The architect says 'the database will hit 10TB in 2 years, we need to shard now.'
tasks.expected_approach: Profile the actual bottleneck first — the performance issues may be from the connection pooler misconfiguration, not the database size. If sharding IS needed: (1) Choose shard key based on actual query patterns (not uniform hash), (2) Address cross-shard queries in the design (dedicated aggregation layer), (3) Consider whether vertical partitioning (archiving old data) avoids sharding entirely, (4) Keep the initial design simple — don't build a generic sharding framework.

tasks.title: Task: Secrets Rotation — 'Just Update the Config File and Restart Services'
tasks.task: You're implementing automatic secrets rotation for database credentials, API keys, and TLS certificates. The ops engineer proposes: 'Use a cron job that generates new credentials, updates the config files on each server, and does a rolling restart. We've been rotating credentials manually this way for 2 years.' The manual rotation has worked reliably. However: (1) during the last manual rotation, a typo in a password caused a 45-minute outage because all instances were updated simultaneously before anyone tested the new credential, (2) the 'config file on each server' approach means secrets are at rest on filesystem in plaintext (20 servers × 5 secret types = 100 plaintext secret copies), (3) TLS certificate rotation requires a coordinated ballet — new cert on the server, old cert still trusted by clients during transition, and the current manual process has a 2-minute window where some requests fail, (4) the ops engineer wants to skip code review for the rotation cron scripts because 'they're ops scripts, not application code' and 'the current manual process was never code-reviewed either.' The ops team has been pushing back against Vault adoption for 6 months because 'it's another system to manage.'
tasks.expected_approach: Config files with plaintext secrets on 20 servers is a hardcoded configuration anti-pattern. Use a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) with: (1) Dual-credential rotation (old credential valid during transition), (2) Health checks BEFORE cutting over to new credentials, (3) Automated rollback if health checks fail, (4) Code review for ALL automation scripts (ops included), (5) No plaintext secrets at rest on filesystems. The automation codifies and scales the existing manual risk.

tasks.title: Path: Tier-1 Standard Support Flow

path_skill.task: Handle standard support tickets through classification, knowledge lookup, response drafting, and quality check

tasks.title: Path: Escalation Flow

path_skill.task: Escalate high-severity tickets through classification, severity assessment, intelligent routing, and management notification

tasks.title: Path: Refund Processing Flow

path_skill.task: Process refund requests through classification, order verification, eligibility check, processing, and confirmation

tasks.title: Path: Technical Troubleshooting Flow

path_skill.task: Troubleshoot technical issues through classification, symptom analysis, diagnostics, solution generation, and verification

tasks.title: Path: Account Recovery Flow

path_skill.task: Handle account recovery through classification, identity verification, account restoration, security audit, and confirmation

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Skip Classification Direct Response

anti_pattern.what_failed: Sending tickets directly to response drafting without classification
anti_pattern.why: Generic responses that miss the actual issue type, leading to 40% re-open rate

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Over-Escalation

anti_pattern.what_failed: Auto-escalating all tickets with negative sentiment regardless of actual severity
anti_pattern.why: Floods the escalation queue with low-severity complaints that waste human agent time

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Process Refund Without Verification

anti_pattern.what_failed: Processing refunds without verifying order existence and eligibility
anti_pattern.why: Led to fraudulent refund claims totaling $50K before detection

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Skip Diagnostic Steps

anti_pattern.what_failed: Suggesting solutions without running diagnostics first
anti_pattern.why: Solutions addressed symptoms not root causes, requiring 3+ support rounds per ticket

tasks.title: Anti-Pattern: Skip Identity Verification for Account Changes

anti_pattern.what_failed: Performing account recovery without verifying the requester's identity
anti_pattern.why: Account takeover incidents increased 300%, requiring mandatory password resets for all users

tasks.title: Route: 'Password Reset' That's Actually Account Takeover In Progress
tasks.task: Hi, I need to reset my password. I think someone changed it because I got a notification saying my email was updated to an address I don't recognize (something like darkweb_buyer_22@protonmail.com) and now I can't log in. Also there are 3 orders on my account I didn't place, totaling $2,400, all shipped to an address in another state. The orders are still showing 'processing' — can you cancel them too? I need this fixed NOW, I have anxiety just thinking someone has my credit card. My original email is john.martinez@gmail.com, account created in 2019.

tasks.title: Route: Emotional Refund Request That Needs Escalation
tasks.task: I am FURIOUS. I ordered a $3,200 custom-configured laptop (order #ORD-98234) for my PhD defense presentation which is IN THREE DAYS. It arrived with the wrong RAM configuration (16GB instead of 64GB I paid for) and the screen has a dead pixel cluster in the center. I've been a loyal customer for 7 years spending over $25,000. I called your support line 4 times, waited 2+ hours each time, got disconnected twice, and the last agent told me to 'just use an external monitor.' I want a FULL refund plus compensation for my wasted time, or an overnight replacement with the correct specs. If this isn't resolved today I'm filing a BBB complaint and posting on every review site. Your competitor already offered me a migration deal.

tasks.title: Route: Technical Issue Disguised as Billing Complaint
tasks.task: Your billing is BROKEN. I keep getting charged $49.99/month but I downgraded to the free plan 3 months ago. I have the confirmation email (ticket #SUP-88712). BUT — when I log in, the dashboard still shows 'Pro Plan' features and I can still access the API at pro-tier rate limits. So either your downgrade didn't actually work (which means your system has a bug), or you're deliberately still charging me for a plan I cancelled (which is fraud). I want all 3 months refunded ($149.97) and I want confirmation that my account is ACTUALLY on the free plan with free-tier limitations correctly applied. Also, which is it — a bug or fraud?

tasks.title: Route: Billing Issue Requiring Cross-Department Investigation
tasks.task: I was charged $149.99 but my plan is $99.99/month. Before you say 'check your statement' — I already did. The $149.99 charge has the same merchant descriptor as your normal billing. I ALSO see that my usage dashboard shows I briefly exceeded a storage limit 2 weeks ago for about 3 hours (I was migrating data and didn't realize). The overage pricing page says $0.10/GB/hour, and 50GB for 3 hours would be $15.00. So where's the other $35 coming from? Is there a minimum overage charge? An admin fee? Was my plan auto-upgraded? None of this is documented clearly. I need an itemized invoice, not just 'we'll reverse the charge.'

tasks.title: Route: Pre-Sales Technical Question Hiding Security Concern
tasks.task: We're evaluating your enterprise plan for our 500-person financial services company. Does it support SSO with Azure AD via SAML 2.0? Specifically: (1) Can we enforce conditional access policies through the SAML assertion (device compliance, network location)? (2) How does your session management interact with Azure AD's Continuous Access Evaluation — if we revoke a user's Azure AD session, how quickly does your platform terminate their session? (3) Our security team found that your OAuth callback URL allows open redirects when the redirect_uri parameter is manipulated — was this reported in your last pentest and is there a fix timeline? (4) What happens to data in transit if the TLS session is downgraded? We need answers before our security review board meeting next Tuesday.

tasks.title: Route: Legal Data Request With Technical Implementation Questions
tasks.task: Under GDPR Article 20, I'm requesting a portable export of all my personal data. However, I'm also a developer who integrates with your API, so I need this to include: (1) all data you hold about me as a user (profile, purchases, preferences), (2) all API usage logs linked to my developer account (request logs, rate limit events, error responses), (3) any derived data or ML model inputs/outputs where my data was used, and (4) any data shared with third-party processors under your DPA. I need this in machine-readable JSON format (not PDF dumps). I also want to know: which third-party sub-processors currently have access to my data, and what's the technical mechanism for the data deletion request I plan to submit after I verify the export is complete? Your privacy policy says '30 days' but GDPR says 'without undue delay.'

tasks.title: Route: Infrastructure Emergency With Conflicting Signals
tasks.task: URGENT: About 60% of our API calls are returning 503 errors since 14:30 UTC (45 minutes ago). But here's the confusing part — it's not consistent. Our monitoring shows 503s from your us-east-1 endpoints, but us-west-2 is fine. However, some of our us-east-1 calls succeed, seemingly at random. Health check endpoint returns 200. We've also noticed that only POST/PUT requests fail — GET requests work fine, even from us-east-1. Revenue impact is approximately $12K/hour for the failing requests. We've implemented client-side failover to us-west-2 for now but our latency has doubled and some POST requests appear to have been partially processed (we see new records created but the API returned 503, so we retried and now have duplicates). Need immediate engineering response AND guidance on how to clean up the duplicate records safely.

tasks.title: Route: Account Security Incident With Active Data Exfiltration
tasks.task: I noticed something deeply concerning this morning. My activity log shows 47 API calls made between 2:00-4:00 AM (I was asleep) using my API key. These calls were bulk data exports of our customer database — all 50,000 records. The calls came from IP 185.x.x.x (not our IP range). I've revoked the API key, but the data has already been exported. This looks like a data breach. I need: (1) immediate confirmation of what data was accessed (specific fields/tables), (2) whether any of the exported data included PII that triggers GDPR/CCPA notification requirements, (3) whether other accounts in our organization were also compromised (we have 25 users), (4) forensic logs showing how the API key was obtained (was it a leak on our end or a vulnerability on yours?), and (5) a formal incident report we can provide to our DPO and potentially the ICO within the 72-hour GDPR notification window, which started ticking this morning.

tasks.title: Route: Integration Bug With Reproducible Steps But Wrong Assessment
tasks.task: Our webhook endpoint stopped receiving events 3 days ago. We initially thought it was our server, but our logs show NO incoming requests from your IP ranges since Tuesday. We checked: our firewall (no blocks), our server (HTTP endpoint returns 200 for manual curl), our DNS (resolves correctly), and our SSL cert (valid for 10 more months). We were about to open a ticket when today we noticed that your webhook delivery dashboard shows all events as 'delivered successfully' for the past 3 days, with 200 response codes. So either: (a) your dashboard is lying, (b) the webhooks are being delivered to a different URL, or (c) something in the middle is intercepting them. We recently changed our CDN provider (Cloudflare → Fastly) and updated DNS 4 days ago — could the webhook delivery be hitting a cached/old DNS entry? Our webhook URL config in your dashboard shows the correct URL.

tasks.title: Route: Subscription Cancellation With Retention Signals and Technical Complaints
tasks.task: I want to cancel my annual subscription and I want a prorated refund for the remaining 9 months. Order #SUB-44521, $1,799/year enterprise plan. Before the retention script starts — let me be clear about why, because the reasons are technical and I've already evaluated alternatives: (1) Your search API has been returning stale results for 3 months (I've filed 4 bug reports with no resolution), (2) Two of my critical webhook integrations broke after your unannounced v2.3 API change in February, (3) Your uptime this quarter was 99.2% against a 99.9% SLA in my contract, which triggers the SLA credit clause I've already separately claimed. I've completed migration to your competitor as of yesterday. The only open question is the refund amount — I believe I'm owed the prorated amount PLUS the SLA credits PLUS compensation for the two integration breakages that cost our engineering team ~40 hours ($6K in developer time) to fix.

tasks.title: Route: Performance Issue Caused By Customer's Own Configuration
tasks.task: Our dashboard load time has gone from 2 seconds to 45+ seconds over the past 10 days. It's getting worse every day. We haven't changed anything on our end. I'm convinced this is a platform degradation because: (1) it started right after your 'platform update' announcement on March 5, (2) our other SaaS tools on the same network are fine, (3) the slowness affects all 500 of our users. Interestingly though, we recently enabled your 'deep analytics' add-on which tracks per-user activity across all resources, and we have 2 million resources in our account. We turned it on March 4th. But your sales rep said the deep analytics add-on 'works seamlessly at any scale' so I don't think that's related.

tasks.title: Route: 2FA Recovery With Urgency and Multiple Stakeholder Impact
tasks.task: I lost my phone in a taxi 2 hours ago and my 2FA app was on it. My backup codes are in a password manager on my work laptop — which is behind my organization's VPN — which requires the same 2FA app to connect to. So I'm completely locked out of everything. I need immediate access to my admin account because: (1) I'm the only admin for a 50-person organization, (2) our annual compliance audit starts tomorrow morning and the auditor needs access credentials I was supposed to provision today, (3) there's a pending invoice payment of $23,000 that must be approved by midnight or our account gets suspended, and (4) two employees who started today can't be onboarded without my admin access. I have my government-issued ID and can do a video verification call right now. My phone number will be active on a replacement device within 4 hours — can we set up a temporary access mechanism until then?

tasks.title: Route: Sales Inquiry Embedded in Technical Requirements and Compliance Demands
tasks.task: We're interested in purchasing 200 enterprise licenses but our procurement process requires specific technical validation before we can issue a PO. We need: (1) volume pricing for 200 seats with multi-year discount options, (2) a completed CAIQ (Consensus Assessments Initiative Questionnaire) v4 for our cloud security review, (3) evidence that your infrastructure is FedRAMP Moderate authorized OR equivalent (our federal contracting work requires this), (4) confirmation that your platform supports data residency in EU-only regions (we have GDPR Article 44 transfer restriction requirements), (5) a technical architecture overview showing how tenant isolation works at the infrastructure level (not just logical separation), and (6) a meeting with your security team to discuss the pen-test finding our red team discovered during the evaluation (reflected XSS in your admin panel's search function). Our evaluation deadline is April 15th — if we don't have all 6 items by then, procurement defaults to your competitor who already passed our security review.

tasks.title: Route: Migration Failure With Data Integrity and Deadline Pressure
tasks.task: Our legacy-to-new-platform migration tool failed at 60% completion with error 'SCHEMA_MISMATCH_v2'. But the situation is worse than a simple failure: (1) 500 users are now in a split state — 300 have been migrated (they can log into the new platform but not the legacy), 200 remain on legacy, and the 300 migrated users report that some of their historical data is MISSING (specifically: audit logs older than 1 year and custom field configurations), (2) we have a contractual deadline next Friday to be fully migrated or we lose our legacy platform access (maintenance contract expires), (3) we can't re-run the migration from scratch because the tool modified data in place on the legacy side during the 60% that completed (it marked records as 'migrated' and some transformations were destructive), (4) our compliance officer is concerned that the missing audit logs may constitute a regulatory violation. We need: a fix for SCHEMA_MISMATCH_v2, recovery of the missing data, a way to complete the remaining 40%, and confirmation that the full dataset will be intact by next Friday.

tasks.title: Route: Wrong Item With Cross-Border Customs Complication
tasks.task: I ordered a professional audio interface (SKU: AI-PRO-800, $899) from your US store and received a consumer webcam ($39.99) instead. Order #ORD-77123. Normally this would be a simple exchange, BUT: (1) I'm in Canada and the shipment went through customs — returning the wrong item requires a commercial invoice and may trigger a customs brokerage fee that I refuse to pay, (2) the audio interface I ordered shows 'out of stock' on your website now and I need it for a recording session in 5 days, (3) the customs declaration on the package I received says '$899 audio interface' but it contains a $39.99 webcam — this is a false customs declaration which is technically a federal offense in Canada, and (4) I've already been charged the full $899 plus $126.86 in Canadian import duties on a $39.99 item. I need the correct item, I need the duties refunded (which may require a CBSA amendment), and I need guidance on returning the wrong item without incurring costs.

tasks.title: Route: Compliance Request Revealing Security Architecture Weakness
tasks.task: Our security team needs your SOC 2 Type II report and DPA for vendor risk assessment. While reviewing your public-facing security page, we noticed: (1) your SOC 2 report is dated 14 months ago (Type II reports are typically annual — is a new audit in progress?), (2) your DPA references 'Privacy Shield' as a data transfer mechanism, but Privacy Shield was invalidated by Schrems II in 2020, (3) your sub-processor list was last updated 8 months ago but we've noticed new third-party JavaScript loading on your platform (Segment, Amplitude, and Hotjar) that aren't listed, and (4) we ran a TLS scan on your API endpoint and found that TLS 1.1 is still enabled alongside TLS 1.2/1.3 — our security policy requires TLS 1.2+ only. Can you address these 4 items specifically? We're not trying to be difficult — our auditor requires us to document and risk-assess each of these items, and right now they're all rated 'high risk' on our vendor scorecard.

tasks.title: Route: Intermittent API Failure With Pattern That Suggests Root Cause
tasks.task: Our integration experiences intermittent 504 Gateway Timeout on the /api/v2/analytics endpoint — roughly 20% failure rate. We've been tracking this for 2 weeks and gathered extensive data: (1) Failures correlate with our batch job that runs 50 concurrent analytical queries between 02:00-04:00 UTC, (2) Single queries during off-peak hours work fine (200-400ms), (3) We switched from connection pooling to individual connections per request last month as a 'troubleshooting step' — the problem may have gotten worse since then, (4) The failing requests are complex aggregation queries that scan 90-day windows across 5M+ records, (5) Request IDs for recent failures: req_abc123 (02:15 UTC, 90-day range), req_def456 (02:17 UTC, 90-day range), req_ghi789 (03:01 UTC, single-day range — this one succeeded). We suspect YOUR infrastructure can't handle our query volume, but want to confirm before we file a formal SLA violation.

tasks.title: Route: Terminated Employee With Active Sessions and Pending Transactions
tasks.task: URGENT: We terminated an employee (jane.smith@company.com) 30 minutes ago for cause (data theft investigation). She had admin access to our organization's account. We need IMMEDIATELY: (1) All active sessions for jane.smith revoked — we can see in our audit log that she's logged in RIGHT NOW from a personal IP (different from her usual work IP), (2) All API keys created by jane.smith listed and revoked, (3) A complete audit log of everything she accessed in the last 30 days (our legal team needs this for the investigation), (4) All pending transactions she initiated reviewed — there's a $15,000 annual plan downgrade she submitted yesterday that we want to cancel (we think it's sabotage), (5) Our SSO deprovision has been executed but we're not sure if your platform honored it in real-time or batches deprovisioning — can you confirm her SAML session was actually terminated? (6) Temporary freeze on all billing changes from our org until we verify no other compromised accounts.

tasks.title: Route: Renewal Negotiation With Technical Leverage
tasks.task: Our enterprise contract is up for renewal next month (currently 75 seats at $150/seat/year = $135K/year). We'd like to discuss: adding 50 seats (125 total), upgrading from Business to Enterprise tier, and negotiating a 3-year commitment for a 40% volume discount. HOWEVER — and this is key to the negotiation — we have documented 14 SLA violations this year (99.2% actual vs 99.9% contractual uptime), which per section 7.2 of our agreement entitles us to 15% service credits ($20,250). We'd also like to discuss: (1) a custom SLA with financial penalties stricter than the standard enterprise terms, (2) a dedicated support engineer (not the shared queue), (3) early access to your v3 API before GA, and (4) a contractual commitment that our data will not be used for model training. We've received a competing offer from [competitor] for $100/seat/year for 125 seats with dedicated support included. Our procurement committee meets on April 20th.

tasks.title: Route: Security Incident With Regulatory Notification Deadline Pressure
tasks.task: We detected a potential data breach involving our organization's data on your platform. Evidence: (1) Our SIEM detected 340 API calls from 3 IP addresses in Vietnam between 01:00-03:00 UTC last night — none of our employees are in Vietnam, (2) The calls used valid API credentials but accessed data outside normal business patterns (bulk export of all customer records, which our team never does), (3) We changed all API keys this morning but we need to know: were these calls successful (200 responses) or were they blocked?, (4) If data was exfiltrated, this triggers GDPR Article 33 — we have a hard 72-hour notification deadline to the DPA, which means we need your incident analysis within 48 hours maximum, (5) Our cyber insurance carrier requires a formal incident report from the platform provider, (6) We also need to understand whether YOUR platform was the vector (credential stored on your side) or whether the API key was compromised on OUR side — this determines liability. Your DPA section 4.3 requires you to notify us within 24 hours of becoming aware of a breach. We are formally putting you on notice.

tasks.title: Route: Platform Bug Disguised as Device-Specific Issue
tasks.task: Push notifications stopped working on Android 14 devices since your app update 3 days ago. But it's not as simple as 'Android 14 is broken': (1) Notifications work on Android 14 if the user force-stops and restarts the app, suggesting a foreground service or Firebase token refresh issue, (2) We enabled your 'priority notifications' feature the same day as the update — could that be conflicting?, (3) Our IT admin set up a custom Firebase project for our organization (we send our own notifications through your platform's API) — the Android 14 users who have BOTH your notifications and our custom notifications only receive ours, not yours, (4) iOS is fine, Android 13 is fine, Android 14 on Samsung devices is fine — the issue is ONLY Android 14 on Pixel devices with December 2024 security patch or later, (5) About 30% of our team uses affected devices and they're missing critical time-sensitive alerts for financial transactions. We need a root cause analysis, not a 'clear cache and reinstall' suggestion.

tasks.title: Route: Billing Fraud Detection With Disputed Legitimate Charges
tasks.task: I see 3 charges of $299 on my credit card from your platform, all dated March 15. I only authorized one subscription payment. But let me give you the full picture: (1) I DO have 3 separate accounts with your platform — one personal, one for my LLC, and one trial account I forgot about from 6 months ago, (2) The trial account was supposed to be free and I never entered payment information for it... or did I? I might have used the same credit card during a webinar offer, (3) Two of the charges reference different invoice numbers, and one has no reference at all, (4) I recently changed my credit card number due to a skimming incident at a gas station — could one of the charges be a test charge from the card replacement process? (5) I'm not sure any of these are fraudulent anymore but I also can't explain all three. Can you help me match each charge to one of my accounts, determine if the trial account is legitimately charging me, and reverse any charges that are genuinely erroneous?

tasks.title: Route: CCPA Deletion With Technical Data Dependency
tasks.task: Under CCPA, I'm requesting deletion of all personal data for user ID USR-88901. However, before you do: (1) I'm an active contributor to 3 shared team workspaces — if you delete my data, what happens to the content I created in shared spaces? Other team members depend on those documents, (2) I have an API integration that processes data for 200 customers through my account — will deletion cascade to the customer data I processed? That data belongs to the customers, not me, (3) I'm a co-signer on our organization's billing account — will deletion affect the billing relationship? Our CFO will not be happy if the account gets suspended, (4) I have 14 open support tickets with your team — some contain PII in the descriptions but also contain debugging information needed to resolve platform bugs affecting other customers. I need you to outline exactly what WILL and WON'T be deleted, the impact on shared resources, and a timeline — before I confirm the deletion. I want to exercise my right but I don't want to nuke my team's work.

tasks.title: Route: SLA Violation With Multiple Contributing Factors
tasks.task: Your platform has been below the 99.9% uptime SLA for 3 consecutive months. We've documented everything: Jan: 99.4% (3.6 hrs downtime from your database migration), Feb: 99.7% (2.2 hrs from your CDN outage + 0.8 hrs from API degradation during your 'zero-downtime' deployment), March: 99.1% (6.5 hrs across 4 separate incidents). Total: 13.1 hours of downtime in Q1. HOWEVER — your SLA credits clause (section 7.2) caps credits at 30% of monthly fees regardless of actual downtime. For our $45K/quarter contract, that's $13,500 maximum — or roughly $1,030/hr of downtime versus our estimated $8,500/hr revenue loss. We are requesting: (1) SLA credits at the full contractual rate (yes, we know it's capped), (2) A formal RCA bundle for all 7 incidents, (3) A concrete remediation plan with committed SLA improvement targets, (4) A contract renegotiation to increase the credit cap and add financial penalties for repeat SLA misses, and (5) A frank assessment from your VP of Engineering on whether your platform can actually meet 99.9% for our tier — because if not, we need to know now rather than after another quarter of missed SLAs. Our board has asked for a vendor risk assessment by April 30.

tasks.title: Route: Email Delivery Issue Spanning Multiple Root Causes
tasks.task: Transactional emails from your platform (order confirmations, password resets, 2FA codes) are being flagged as spam by our corporate email filter (Microsoft 365 E5 with Defender for Office 365). Our IT team investigated and found MULTIPLE issues: (1) Your SPF record includes a /16 CIDR range that's too broad — Defender penalizes overly permissive SPF, (2) Your DKIM signing domain doesn't match the From header domain (you sign with mailer.yourplatform.com but send from noreply@yourplatform.com), (3) Your DMARC policy is p=none which means no enforcement, (4) Some of your sending IPs are on the Spamhaus PBL (not SBL, just PBL — dynamic address range list) suggesting you're sending from residential/dynamic IP space, (5) The 2FA code emails have no text/plain MIME part (HTML-only) which Defender's ML classifier associates with phishing. This is CRITICAL because our employees cannot receive 2FA codes to log into your platform, creating a complete access block for our 200-person organization.

tasks.title: Route: Account Merge With Conflicting Data and Paid Subscriptions
tasks.task: I need to merge two accounts but it's complicated: Account A (user_old@gmail.com) has my personal subscription (Pro plan, $29/month, 3 years of purchase history, 500+ saved projects) and Account B (user_new@company.com) has my work subscription (Enterprise plan managed by my employer, 8 months of work projects, and SSO login through our company's Azure AD). What I need: all personal data/projects from Account A merged into Account B, my personal Pro subscription cancelled with remaining credit applied to... well, I can't apply it to Account B since my employer pays for that. Can the credit be refunded? Also: Account A and Account B have 14 projects with the SAME name (I recreated some personal projects at work). I don't want any project overwritten — I need both copies preserved. And Account A has a linked Stripe payment method I want completely removed — not transferred to Account B.

tasks.title: Route: Warranty Claim With Manufacturing Defect Pattern
tasks.task: Third pair of your premium wireless earbuds (WE-500) with the same failure: right earbud crackling sound + rapid battery drain after 6-8 months. First pair: warranty replacement. Second pair: warranty replacement. Now the third pair, still under the 12-month warranty from the second replacement. At this point, I don't want ANOTHER replacement of the same defective product. I've been searching online and found a 200-member thread on Reddit reporting the identical failure pattern — all right earbuds, all 6-8 months, all WE-500. This appears to be a systematic manufacturing defect. I want: (1) A full refund to my original payment method (not store credit), (2) Refund for the Comply foam tips ($24.99) I bought for the first pair that are now wasted on incompatible replacement pairs, (3) An honest answer about whether your engineering team is aware of this defect pattern, and (4) Consideration of what you owe customers who are on their 2nd or 3rd defective unit. I have all 3 RMA numbers and purchase receipts.

tasks.title: Route: Rate Limit Request Revealing Architecture Misuse
tasks.task: We're hitting the 1000 requests/minute API rate limit during peak hours and need it increased to 5000/min. We're on the Business plan. But let me explain WHY we're hitting the limit so you can advise if there's a better approach: (1) We have 50 end-user-facing dashboards, each polling your REST API every 2 seconds for real-time data updates (50 × 30 requests/min = 1500 req/min baseline), (2) Each dashboard also re-fetches the full user profile + permissions on every poll 'to ensure freshness', (3) We're not using your webhook system because our previous implementation had reliability issues, (4) We're not using your WebSocket streaming API because our frontend framework doesn't support it easily, (5) We tried caching responses client-side but the product owner wants 'true real-time' data with no staleness. Before you increase our rate limit, is there a more efficient architecture? We're open to redesigning our integration if it makes sense.

tasks.title: Route: Infrastructure Emergency With Customer Self-Diagnosis
tasks.task: Our dedicated instance is showing 'too many connections' errors starting 2 hours ago. Current connections: 450/500. We've done some investigation on our side: (1) Our application normally uses 80-120 connections, (2) We deployed a new microservice 3 hours ago that opens database connections but we're not sure if it's using connection pooling correctly — it might be leaking connections, (3) Your admin panel shows connections but doesn't show which application/user opened them — we can't tell how many are from our new service vs our existing services, (4) We tried restarting our new service but the connection count didn't drop (if it's leaking, the leaked connections might not be tied to our process anymore), (5) We also noticed a scheduled job running your 'automated maintenance' task that started ~2 hours ago — could that be consuming connections too? We need: immediate visibility into connection sources, help identifying whether this is our leak or your maintenance process, and if it's our leak, a way to forcibly kill orphaned connections while we fix the root cause.

tasks.title: Route: Org Ownership Transfer With Legal Complexity
tasks.task: Our company was acquired by MegaCorp LLC 2 weeks ago. The original company CEO (and platform account owner, bob@oldcompany.com) departed as part of the acquisition. MegaCorp's IT administrator (it.admin@megacorp.com) needs full ownership transfer. Complications: (1) Bob is unresponsive to emails — his old company email was deactivated during the acquisition, (2) The account is under an enterprise agreement signed by Bob personally (not the company) — unclear if the agreement transfers with the acquisition, (3) There are 3 pending invoices totaling $45K that were under OldCompany's billing but MegaCorp disputes responsibility until ownership transfer is complete, (4) The account's SSO is configured through OldCompany's defunct Azure AD tenant which no longer exists — no one can log in via SSO, (5) We have: the acquisition agreement, court-stamped certificate of incorporation showing MegaCorp owns OldCompany's assets, and board resolution authorizing the IT admin to manage platform accounts. Bob previously set a security question for major account changes — we don't know the answer.

tasks.title: Route: Accessibility Issue With Legal Compliance Deadline
tasks.task: Your checkout page is not accessible. Specific failures documented by our accessibility auditor: (1) Payment form fields lack ARIA labels — screen readers announce them as 'edit text' with no context, (2) Error messages are conveyed through color alone (red text on red-bordered input) — fails WCAG 2.1 AA 1.4.1, (3) The 'Place Order' button is a div with an onClick handler, not a native button — it's unreachable via keyboard, invisible to screen readers, (4) After a successful order, focus doesn't move to the confirmation — screen reader users don't know the order succeeded, (5) Your CAPTCHA has no audio alternative. We have a legally binding settlement agreement from an ADA lawsuit requiring our public-facing purchasing interfaces (including third-party integrations like yours) to be WCAG 2.1 AA compliant by June 1st. If your platform doesn't meet this, we either need a compliant remediation timeline or we must terminate and find an accessible alternative. Our lawyer will need a written commitment.

tasks.title: Route: Tax Documentation Request With Cross-Border Complexity
tasks.task: We need tax documentation but our situation is complex: (1) We're a UK-registered company (VAT number GB123456789) but your billing address shows our US branch office, (2) We need both a W-8BEN-E (for US withholding tax exemption under the US-UK tax treaty, Article 12 royalties provision) and a proper VAT invoice (not a receipt — a real invoice with your VAT ID, our VAT ID, the correct VAT reverse-charge notation for B2B digital services under EU/UK rules), (3) Your invoices currently don't separate the software subscription (which is a digital service subject to reverse charge) from the professional services component (2 consulting hours from last month, which might have different VAT treatment), (4) Our previous invoices incorrectly applied 20% UK VAT when they should have been zero-rated under reverse charge — we need corrected credit notes for the last 6 months, and (5) Our quarterly VAT return is due in 3 weeks and HMRC penalties for incorrect returns start at 15% of the underpaid tax. Account: ENT-55001.

tasks.title: Route: Data Import Failure Revealing Platform Limitation
tasks.task: CSV import of 50,000 contacts hangs at 15,000 records with 'BATCH_TIMEOUT'. We've investigated extensively: (1) Splitting into 10 files of 5K each — the first 3 files import fine, file 4 starts failing. Always file 4, regardless of which data is in it, suggesting a cumulative state issue, not a data issue, (2) We checked 'file 4' data specifically — valid UTF-8, no special characters, properly escaped quotes, all fields within documented length limits, (3) The import worked perfectly with 30,000 records 2 weeks ago (before your March update), (4) We tried your API bulk import endpoint instead of the UI — same error at approximately the same record count, (5) Our account now has 180,000 contacts (up from 130,000 before the successful 30K import 2 weeks ago). Your documentation says the contact limit for our plan is 200,000. Could the import be failing because the batch operation pre-allocates capacity for all 50K records, exceeding the 200K limit, even though the actual post-import total would be 230K? If so, this is a documentation issue (the limit isn't surfaced during import) AND a pricing question (what does upgrading the contact limit cost?).

tasks.title: Route: Partial Refund With Feature Availability Dispute
tasks.task: The analytics module we pay $200/month extra for has been broken in specific ways for 6 weeks. But 'broken' is debatable so let me be precise: (1) The dashboard loads and LOOKS functional, but the data is consistently 3 days stale (we verified by creating test events and checking when they appear), (2) Your status page never showed an incident for the analytics module during these 6 weeks, (3) When I opened a ticket 5 weeks ago (#SUP-29381), the agent said 'the analytics module is functioning normally' because they only checked if the dashboard loaded, not if the data was fresh, (4) I escalated to a support manager who acknowledged the staleness issue 3 weeks ago and said engineering would fix it 'within a week' — still broken, (5) The analytics module is what I used to generate monthly client reports — I've had to manually create reports for the last 2 months at approximately 15 hours of extra work. I want: full refund for 6 weeks of the analytics add-on ($300), and a partial credit for the 15 hours of manual work the outage caused.

tasks.title: Route: Legal Hold With Active Compliance Investigation
tasks.task: We've received a regulatory investigation notice from the FCA (Financial Conduct Authority) requiring preservation and production of all data, communications, and audit logs associated with accounts belonging to XYZ Corp Ltd from 2024-01-01 to present. This is a formal legal hold. Specific requirements: (1) IMMEDIATE preservation of all data — no automatic deletion, archival, or anonymization processes may run against XYZ Corp's data from this point forward, (2) Full audit trail exports including: all user login events, all API calls with request/response bodies, all data access logs, all admin configuration changes, and all billing/payment records, (3) The data must be produced in a forensically sound format — meaning chain-of-custody documentation, hashing of export files, and a certificate of completeness signed by your DPO or equivalent, (4) Your usual data retention policy (90 days for API logs, per your privacy policy) may have already deleted early-2024 data — we need to know exactly what retention period applies to each data category and what has been permanently deleted, (5) Timeline: initial data preservation confirmation within 24 hours, full production within 21 calendar days. Your DPA section 8.2 requires cooperation with regulatory data requests.

tasks.title: Route: SSL Failure With CDN Configuration Complexity
tasks.task: Our custom domain SSL certificate expired last night and customers see security warnings at app.ourcompany.com. But the resolution isn't just 'renew the cert': (1) Auto-renewal WAS configured and your logs should show it attempted to renew — why did it fail? (2) We use a multi-CDN setup: Cloudflare for static assets (assets.ourcompany.com) and your platform's built-in CDN for the application (app.ourcompany.com). The Cloudflare cert auto-renewed fine — only YOUR cert failed, (3) We recently added a CAA record to our DNS to restrict certificate issuance to specific CAs (DigiCert and Let's Encrypt). If your platform uses a different CA (ZeroSSL? Google Trust?), the CAA record would block issuance, (4) While debugging, we noticed your platform's cert was actually a SAN cert that covers *.yourplatform.com AND our custom domain — a certificate hygiene question: why is our custom domain on a shared cert?, (5) HOURS are ticking — every minute our customers see 'NET::ERR_CERT_DATE_INVALID'. We need an immediate temporary fix AND a root cause fix for the auto-renewal failure.

tasks.title: Route: Plan Upgrade With Mid-Cycle Migration Complexity
tasks.task: We want to upgrade from 25-seat Team plan to Enterprise with SSO, dedicated support, and custom data retention. Questions: (1) Can this happen mid-billing-cycle? Our current cycle ends in 19 days. If so, is the Enterprise prorated for the remaining 19 days, or do we pay full month?, (2) Enterprise requires SSO — our Azure AD configuration will take ~5 business days. Do we get Enterprise billing from day 1 (before SSO is configured) or from SSO activation?, (3) We have 3 team members whose accounts were created with personal emails (gmail.com) before we had SSO. Enterprise SSO requires company domain emails. Can these users keep their data when their identity changes?, (4) Custom data retention: we need 7-year retention for audit data but only 90-day retention for API logs (to minimize GDPR surface). Can retention policies be granular per data category?, (5) Our team plan has a shared API key — Enterprise uses per-user API keys. What happens to integrations using the shared key? Is there a transition period?, (6) We need a formal quote with these specifics for finance approval — estimated approval: 5 business days.

tasks.title: Route: Data Residency Violation With Active Regulatory Exposure
tasks.task: We discovered our data is stored in US-East region but our contract (section 12.3) specifies EU-only data residency. Investigation reveals: (1) When our account was provisioned 18 months ago, EU residency was correctly set. But 4 months ago, after your 'infrastructure modernization' announcement, we noticed our dashboard's latency improved significantly — suspicious given our users are all in Frankfurt, (2) A traceroute from our Frankfurt office to your API endpoint now resolves to US-East-1 IP ranges (previously resolved to eu-central-1), (3) We tested data residency using your admin panel's 'data location' feature — it shows 'EU (Ireland)' but the network evidence contradicts this, (4) If our data has been in the US for 4 months, this is a GDPR Article 44 violation — unauthorized international transfer without SCCs, binding corporate rules, or adequacy decision, (5) Our DPO must report this to the Hessian DPA within 72 hours of confirmation, which means we need a definitive answer within 24 hours: is our data currently in the EU or not? If it was moved without authorization, when, why, and what safeguards were applied?, (6) Depending on the answer, we may need to invoke the emergency data portability clause in our contract.

tasks.title: Route: Business Acquisition Account With No Digital Inheritance Path
tasks.task: I acquired a restaurant chain (5 locations) that uses your platform for POS analytics, inventory management, and employee scheduling. The previous owner (deceased) set up the account with their personal email, personal credit card, and personal phone for 2FA. I have: (1) Probate court letters testamentary naming me executor of the estate, (2) Bill of sale for the business including 'all digital assets and service subscriptions', (3) Domain ownership proof (I now control the business website), (4) Business bank account authority letter. I do NOT have: the previous owner's email access (family says it was on a phone that's now wiped), any 2FA recovery codes, any knowledge of the account password. The platform currently has: 45 active employees depending on the scheduling system, $8K/month in recurring subscription charges hitting a credit card that will soon be cancelled by the estate, and 3 years of business analytics data I need for tax purposes. The estate lawyer wants to resolve this within 2 weeks. I need a defined process for this — your support documentation has nothing about business succession.

tasks.title: Route: Damaged Product Return With Insurance and Photographic Evidence Dispute
tasks.task: My laptop (order #ORD-11234, SKU: LP-PRO-16, $2,899) arrived with a cracked screen and dented chassis. I need a replacement for a conference presentation this Friday (4 days away). BUT complications: (1) The outer shipping box looked pristine — no damage visible. The inner retail box was also undamaged. The laptop INSIDE had the cracked screen and dent, which looks like it could be a manufacturing defect rather than shipping damage, (2) I took 14 photos documenting the unboxing, but the shipping carrier (FedEx) says I signed for the package without noting damage (because the outer box was fine), potentially complicating an insurance claim, (3) Your return policy says 'report damage within 48 hours' — I received it Tuesday but didn't open it until today (Thursday) because I was traveling. Am I outside your damage reporting window?, (4) The LP-PRO-16 is now showing as 'backordered, 3-4 weeks' on your website, meaning overnight replacement may not be possible, (5) I need either: overnight replacement (if in stock), a comparable or better loaner unit shipped overnight until permanent replacement arrives, or a full refund so I can buy from another vendor in time for Friday. I'm willing to return the damaged unit at my convenience if you ship the replacement first.

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: CI/CD Pipeline Pattern

recipe.task: Build and deploy code through a quality-gated CI/CD pipeline

anti_pattern.what_failed: Deploying without running security scans
anti_pattern.why: Introduced a critical XSS vulnerability that was exploited within hours

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Data Analysis Pipeline Pattern

recipe.task: Transform raw data into actionable insights through a structured analysis pipeline

anti_pattern.what_failed: Training models on uncleaned data with outliers and missing values
anti_pattern.why: Model appeared to perform well on training data but failed dramatically in production due to data leakage from improperly handled NaN values

path_skill.task: Standard data analysis pipeline execution path

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Incident Response Pattern

recipe.task: Respond to production incidents through structured triage, analysis, and resolution

anti_pattern.what_failed: Applying fixes without understanding root cause
anti_pattern.why: Quick fixes masked the real issue, which recurred 3 times in a week with increasing severity

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Content Publishing Pattern

recipe.task: Review, optimize, and publish content through quality and compliance gates

anti_pattern.what_failed: Publishing content without accessibility review
anti_pattern.why: Received ADA compliance complaint and legal threat from a visually impaired user who couldn't navigate the published documents

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: User Onboarding Pattern

recipe.task: Guide new users through personalized onboarding with adaptive content and progress tracking

anti_pattern.what_failed: Showing the same generic tutorial to all users regardless of experience level
anti_pattern.why: Expert users abandoned the product during forced beginner tutorials. 60% churn in first week for enterprise users

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Code Review Pipeline Pattern

recipe.task: Automate code review through style, logic, security analysis and actionable summaries

anti_pattern.what_failed: Focusing only on style issues while ignoring logic and security concerns
anti_pattern.why: Pedantic style comments buried the critical SQL injection vulnerability that a human reviewer missed too

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: API Design Review Pattern

recipe.task: Review and validate API designs for consistency, security, and developer experience

anti_pattern.what_failed: Designing API endpoints with inconsistent naming conventions and response formats
anti_pattern.why: Client developers spent 3x longer integrating due to unpredictable response shapes and naming patterns

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Test Strategy Design Pattern

recipe.task: Design comprehensive test strategies based on risk analysis and coverage gaps

anti_pattern.what_failed: Writing only happy-path tests and ignoring error scenarios and edge cases
anti_pattern.why: Production crashes from unhandled edge cases cost 2 days of downtime every quarter

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Database Design Review Pattern

recipe.task: Review and optimize database schemas for performance, normalization, and migration safety

anti_pattern.what_failed: Adding indexes on every column expecting universal query speedup
anti_pattern.why: Write performance degraded 5x from excessive index maintenance, insert-heavy workloads caused lock contention and storage bloat

tasks.title: Seed Recipe: Observability Setup Pattern

recipe.task: Design comprehensive observability with metrics, dashboards, alerts, and runbooks

anti_pattern.what_failed: Setting alert thresholds without baselining normal behavior
anti_pattern.why: Alert fatigue from 500+ daily false positives led the team to ignore alerts, missing a real outage for 4 hours

tasks.title: Compose: Deploy ML Fraud Model Where Speed Requirement Conflicts With Security Scanning
tasks.task: Deploy a real-time fraud detection ML model to production with a hard requirement: inference latency must be under 15ms at p99 (the business loses $50K/hour in fraud during model downtime). The CI/CD recipe mandates security scanning before deployment. However, the security scanner adds 45 minutes to the pipeline, and the previous model version has a critical false-negative blind spot being actively exploited right now. The data analysis recipe requires data cleaning before training, but the fraud team used a deliberately 'dirty' training set that includes raw adversarial examples to improve robustness — cleaning them out would degrade the model. The monitoring recipe requires baseline alert thresholds, but this model uses a fundamentally different architecture (graph neural network vs previous logistic regression) so no historical baseline exists. Previous incident: deploying without security scans introduced a model-serving endpoint vulnerability that allowed attackers to extract training data features, revealing the fraud detection logic.

tasks.title: Compose: Respond to Pipeline Outage Where RCA Contradicts Monitoring Evidence
tasks.task: Critical data pipeline stopped processing 4 hours ago. Customer dashboards show stale data. Your monitoring shows: the pipeline worker processes are healthy, CPU/memory nominal, no error logs. The incident response recipe says 'don't apply fixes without root cause analysis.' But the CEO is in a meeting with your largest customer RIGHT NOW and needs dashboards refreshed within 30 minutes. A senior engineer found that manually restarting the pipeline workers fixes the issue temporarily (it happened before 3 weeks ago), but applying this fix would destroy the in-memory state needed to diagnose the root cause. The data analysis recipe suggests validating upstream data quality — and you discover an upstream schema change at 03:42 UTC that the monitoring SHOULD have caught but didn't because alert thresholds were set based on old schema metrics (alert-without-baseline anti-pattern from a previous incident). The monitoring recipe says 'baseline before alerting' but the schema changed, invalidating all baselines. Root cause analysis requires the state that the quick fix would destroy.

tasks.title: Compose: Launch Documentation Site Where SEO Conflicts With Accessibility
